element.name: accessDoorOrPanel
element.definition: The element <accessDoorOrPanel> contains information about doors or panels to be opened for getting access to the electrical equipment.
element.title: Access door or panel

element.name: accessFrom
element.definition: The element <accessFrom> enables specifying the zone(s) and/or access point(s) from which the functional item can be reached.
element.title: Accesses to the functional item

element.name: accessory
element.definition:
The element <accessory> is contained in the list of accessories element <accessoryGroup>. The list of accessories is a child element of the list of the standard parts element <standardPartGroup>.
It contains information concerning the properties of accessories used in the Product's wiring.
element.title: Element <accessory> and child elements

element.name: accessoryGroup
element.definition: the list of accessories
element.title: Electrical standard parts

element.name: accessPoint
element.definition: The element <accessPoint> contains information that describes the access point and provides further information related to the access point based on the Product configuration.
element.title: Alternate access point

element.name: accessPointAlts
element.definition: The element <accessPointAlts> is a group of access point alternates.
element.title: Access point alternate group

element.name: accessPointIdent
element.definition: The element <accessPointIdent>, contrary to the attribute id, semantically identifies the access point as a result of its attributes.
element.title: Access point identifier

element.name: accessPointRef
element.definition:
The element <accessPointRef> describe/identify any access points such as panels, doors and hatches. The identifier of the access point can be given either thru the attribute accessPointNumber and/or the elements <name> and <refs>. For the use of the element <accessPointRef> in connection with common information repositories, refer to Chap 4.13.1 and Chap 3.9.5.2.11.5.
This element is available in the product management data within the element <productionMaintData> and <reqCondCircuitBreaker> of preliminary requirements (refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.1.9) and within the element <para> of descriptive data modules.
element.title: Access point

element.name: accessPointRefGroup
element.definition:
The element <accessPointRefGroup> provides relationships and the types of relationships with other access points (eg, subaccess points).
The types of relationships depend on the configuration of the Product. As a result, this element can be found at both container and alternate levels.
element.title: References to other access points

element.name: accessPointRepository
element.definition: The element <accessPointRepository> provides a list of access points and their associated properties.
element.title: Access point repository

element.name: accessPointSpec
element.definition:
The element <accessPointSpec> contains the associated properties for one access point.
Some properties of the access point can vary from one product instance to another. Such properties are managed under the access point alternates (element <accessPointAlts>), which can contain a reference to an applicability annotation. Refer to Chap 4.13.3 for details on the alternates concept.
For some properties appearing at both levels, the following rule must be applied: if the property is fulfilled in the alternate element, it supersedes the generic value stored in the parent element.
element.title: Access point specification

element.name: accessPointType
element.definition: The element <accessPointType> is used to specify, thru its attribute, whether the access point is a panel, a door, an electrical panel or any other type of access point.
element.title: Access point type

element.name: accessTo
element.definition: The element <accessTo> allows listing items that can be reached from the access point. Those items can be functional items or other items.
element.title: Items reachable from the access point

element.name: acronym
element.definition:
The element <acronym> contains an acronym and its definition when the acronym is first introduced.
Once an acronym has been defined in text using the element <acronym>, it can be reused, and its definition be referred to, multiple times.
element.title: Acronym

element.name: acronymDefinition
element.definition: The element <acronymDefinition> contains the definition of the acronym when that acronym is first used. The use of this element without its parent element <acronym> and its sibling element <acronymTerm> is not sensible.
element.title: Acronym definition

element.name: acronymTerm
element.definition: The element <acronymTerm> contains the acronym itself and a reference to its definition.
element.title: Acronym term

element.name: action
element.definition: The element <action> contains an action in the isolation procedure.
element.title: Action

element.name: actionAlts
element.definition: The element <actionAlts> is an alternates group that provides the capability to group several alternate solutions of the action. Refer to Chap 4.13.3 for information related to the use of alternates group in data module content.
element.title: Action alternates group

element.name: additionalModification
element.definition: The element <additionalModification> contains an indication that a modification is additionally covered.
element.title: Additional modification

element.name: address
element.definition: The element <address> contains the postal address from where the comment/response is sent.
element.title: Address

element.name: alteredFromPart
element.definition:
The element <alteredFromPart> provides one or several parts from which the defined part has been modified. The altered from parts can be used on condition that a modification has been performed. The description of the modification can be provided.
The altered from part can be identified by:
− using the part number and manufacturer code (allowing then to refer to the part common information repository data module, if it is implemented) or
− using the reference to the catalog sequence number where the part is described
element.title: Altered from parts

element.name: alteredFromPartDescr
element.definition: The element <alteredFromPartDescr> contains the modification to be performed on one altered from part.
element.title: Altered from part modification

element.name: altIdent
element.definition: The alternative identification element <altIdent> is used for additional identification of a harness or an electrical equipment. An alternative identification can contain a manufacturer part number and the related manufacturer code.
element.title: Alternative identification

element.name: altIdentGroup
element.definition: The list of alternative identifications element <altIdentGroup> is used as a container for the subordinate alternative identification elements.
element.title: Alternative identifications

element.name: amperage
element.definition: The element <amperage> contains the amperage information of wire material or circuit breaker.
element.title: Amperage

element.name: answer
element.definition: The element <answer> can be used to insert comments on a DMRL entry. This element must not be used in a CSL, and must only be used for data module entries. Refer to default BREX rules BREX-S1-00007 and BREX-S1-00008.
element.title: Data management requirement list answer

element.name: applic
element.definition: The element <applic> contains the applicability limitations and any text that is displayed (or not) to the user. It is populated by the technical author or generated by the production system during initial authoring and updating activity of the data module.
element.title: Applicability model

element.name: applicabilitySegment
element.definition: The element <applicabilitySegment> identifies an S2000M applicability segment.
element.title: Applicability segment

element.name: applicCrossRefTable
element.definition: The element <applicCrossRefTable> is used to provide the content of the ACT data module or the ACT Catalog data module.
element.title: Applicability cross-reference table

element.name: applicCrossRefTableCatalog
element.definition:
The element <applicCrossRefTableCatalog> contains the whole ACT catalog. This is the parent element for the ACT catalog data module. The ACT catalog contains two parts:
− The list of ACT data modules in the projects
− The relationships between the different ACT/CCT set
element.title: Applicability cross-reference table catalog

element.name: applicCrossRefTableRef
element.definition: The element <applicCrossRefTableRef> contains a pointer to the Applicability Cross-reference Table (ACT) data module that applies to the data module or publication module. Applicability and the ACT data module and related aspects are described in Chap 3.9.5.3.1, Chap 4.14, and Chap 7.8.
element.title: Applicability cross-reference table reference

element.name: applicIdent
element.definition: The element <applicIdent> identifies an applicability annotation. It must be used by projects or organizations, which do not externalize applicability.
element.title: Applicability annotation identifier

element.name: applicRef
element.definition: The element <applicRef> contains the reference to an applicability annotation in an applicability annotations CIR data module. It is used for externalizing applicability annotations. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.11.12.
element.title: References to externalized applicability annotations

element.name: applicRefIdent
element.definition: The element <applicRefIdent> identifies a reference to an externalized applicability annotation. It must be used by projects or organizations, which do externalize applicability.
element.title: Reference to externalized applicability annotation identifier

element.name: applicRepository
element.definition: The element <applicRepository> contains a list of externalized applicability annotations used in the data modules.
element.title: Applicability annotations repository

element.name: applicSpec
element.definition: The element <applicSpec> represents one applicability annotation.
element.title: Applicability annotation specification

element.name: applicSpecIdent
element.definition: The element <applicSpecIdent> uniquely identifies the applicability annotation thru its attribute applicIdentValue. This identifier must be used to refer from the data modules where the applicability annotation is externalized (element <applicRef>) to the externalized applicability annotation in the applicability annotations CIR data module.
element.title: Applicability annotation identifier

element.name: arc
element.title: xlink element, see W3C Recommendation: XML Linking Language (Xlink) Version 1.1

element.name: assert
element.definition:
The element <assert> provides for a single test for the computable branch of the applicability annotation. The assert annotation specifies the product attribute or condition to test and a set of values and/or ranges to test against. The result of evaluating an assert annotation will be a Boolean value of "true" or "false".
In the case where an assert cannot be evaluated due to lack of a value to test against or the case where only a textual annotation is provided (not referencing a product attribute or condition) then the result must be considered the Boolean value "true".
The element <assert> can be used to represent a textual applicability annotation which does not have an associated product attribute or condition, meaning there is no test to perform. Examples of textual annotations include "volcanic conditions" and "wind speed over 30 knots". The textual content of element <assert> is used to represent the annotation and the attributes applicPropertyIdent, applicPropertyType and applicPropertyValues are not used.
element.title: Single applicability test - assert

element.name: assertion
element.definition: The element <assertion> assigns a value to a local variable or global property.
element.title: Assertion

element.name: assign
element.definition: The element <assign> associates a value with a product attribute or condition.
element.title: Assign

element.name: associate
element.definition:
The element <associate> is used to associate an external assertion (product attribute, product attribute value or condition as defined within a specific ACT data module) to one or several other ones. There are two types of association:
The association is made between the external assertion as defined as the child of the product relationship (element <productDefinitionRelationship>) and the external assertion(s) as defined under the association (element <association>)
Example:
Product definition relationship
External assertion #1
Associate Type = "Alias"
External assertion #2
means that External assertion #1 and #2 are linked by an "alias" association.
element.title: Associating external assertions

element.name: assocWarningMalfunction
element.definition: The associated fault (warning or malfunction) which is part of the current correlated fault.
element.title: Associated warning or malfunction

element.name: assocWarningMalfunctionAlts
element.definition: The element <assocWarningMalfunctionAlts> is an alternates group that provides the capability to group several alternate solutions of element <assocWarningMalfunction>. Refer to Chap 4.13.3 for information related to the use of alternates group in data module content.
element.title: Associated warning or malfunction alternates group

element.name: assy
element.definition: The electrical equipment assembly information element <assy> contains subordinate assembly information for the electrical equipment in coded form, and references detailed assembly instructions.
element.title: Assembly information

element.name: assyInstruction
element.definition: The element <assyInstruction> is used as a container for the subordinate electrical equipment and connector assembly information.
element.title: Assembly instructions

element.name: attachmentRef
element.definition:
The element <attachmentRef> contains the file name of the attachment.
The full file name consists of the prefix "COM", the comment code (ie, the attributes of the element <commentCode>) and the attachment number, all items separated by dashes. The file name ends with a separation character (usually ".") and the file type extension (eg, PDF, ZIP, XLS).
COM-YY-YYYYY-XXXX-NNNNN-A-MM.EXT
or
COM-YYYYYYYYYYYYYY-YYYYY-XXXX-NNNNN-A-MM.EXT
Where:
− "MM" is the attachment number starting with "01"
− "EXT" is the file type extension
Note
The allowed file types must be defined by the project or the organization and must be supported by the viewing systems.
element.title: Reference to an attachment

element.name: attachStoreShipPart
element.definition: The element <attachStoreShipPart> indicates if an item is an attaching, storage or shipping part.
element.title: Attaching storage or shipping part

element.name: attentionListItemPara
element.definition: The element <attentionListItemPara> contains the list item paragraphs.
element.title: Attention list item para

element.name: attentionRandomList
element.definition:
The element <attentionRandomList> contains the list items.
There are two types of attention random lists:
− simple - recognized by the value ""pf01"" of the attribute listItemPrefix giving just an indented item without a prefi
x − unordered - the lists recognized by the value ""pf03"" or ""pf07"" of the attribute listItemPrefix giving the following prefix: [-] or [●], respectively
The difference between a simple random list and an unordered random list becomes visible when presented. Refer to Chap 6.2.2.
Only one level of attention random lists is allowed.
element.title: Attention random list

element.name: attentionRandomListItem
element.definition:
The element <attentionRandomListItem> contains one list item of an attention random list.
Each list item can consist of one or more paragraphs.
element.title: Attention random list item

element.name: attentionSequentialList
element.definition:
The element <attentionSequentialList> contains an attention sequential (ordered) list in a note. An attention sequential list, when presented, has numbers before the list items. Refer to Chap 6.2.2.
In the attention sequential list, a list item can consist of one or more paragraphs.
A sequential list, when presented, is numbered with arabic numerals. Refer to Chap 6.2.2.
Only one attention sequential list is allowed in a Note.
Attention sequential lists are limited to one level.
element.title: Attention sequential list

element.name: attentionSequentialListItem
element.definition: The element <attentionSequentialListItem> contains one list item of an attention sequential list item and can consist of one or more paragraphs.
element.title: Attention sequential list item

element.name: attenuation
element.definition: The element <attenuation> contains the attenuation information of wire material.
element.title: Attenuation

element.name: authorityExceptions
element.definition: The element <authorityExceptions> contains deviations of the data module content from the standard given in the authority block for configurations.
element.title: Authority exceptions

element.name: authorityInfo
element.definition: The element <authorityInfo> contains the authority that owns the source data from which the data module is written. If codes are used, they are determined by the project.
element.title: Authority information

element.name: authorityInfoAndTp
element.definition: The element <authorityInfoAndTp> contains both the authority block information and the technical publications baseline when it is appropriate to use both.
element.title: Authority block and technical publications information

element.name: authorityNotes
element.definition: The element <authorityNotes> contains additional information that can be necessary to explain preceding entries. Whenever a data module is only applicable to one or a range of serial numbers, this is stated here. It is recommended that, where applicable, the titles of documents be given under authority exceptions. The notes are also used for recording deficiency reports, special technical orders, service bulletins, explanations of excluded and additional modifications, and similar documents.
element.title: Authority notes

element.name: authorization
element.definition: to be found in DDN schema
element.title: authorization

element.name: authorizationStatus
element.definition: The element <authorizationStatus> contains the authorization status of the supply requirement alternative.
element.title: Authorization status

element.name: barCode
element.definition:
The element <barCode> contains the barcode information. Several barcodes can be used.
The first appearance of the element <barCode> will by default be the one presented on the title page. Refer to Chap 6.2.3.1.
element.title: Barcode

element.name: barCodeCode
element.definition: The element <barCodeCode> contains the barcode value and the used symbology.
element.title: Barcode value and symbology

element.name: barCodeSymbol
element.definition: The element <barCodeSymbol> contains the graphical representation of the barcode.
element.title: Barcode symbol

element.name: basicCorrelatedFaults
element.definition: The element <basicCorrelatedFaults> contains the list of basic faults (messages and/or warnings) that have been correlated.
element.title: Messages and warnings

element.name: behavior
element.definition: The element <behavior> contains the appearance (behavior) of the destination data module or a fragment of the destination data module.
element.title: Link behavior information

element.name: bitMessage
element.definition: The basic fault (computerized message or warning/malfunction) which is part of the current correlated fault.
element.title: Computerized message from built-in test equipment

element.name: bitMessageAlts
element.definition: The element <bitMessageAlts> is an alternates group that provides the capability to group several alternate solutions of element <bitMessage>. Refer to Chap 4.13.3 for information related to the use of alternates group in data module content.
element.title: Computerized message from built-in test equipment alternates group

element.name: blockGroupingValue
element.definition:
The element <blockGroupingValue> is only used for equipment with internal logic. It provides grouping information for contacts that belong to the same block of a termination module assembly or the same pole of a switch or a relay.
It must contain an identical alphanumerical value for all these contacts.
element.title: Block grouping information

element.name: booleanFunction
element.definition:
The element <booleanFunction> is a Boolean function that returns the value "true" or "false" against a single expression in the Process DM. The Boolean function evaluates the element <expression> based on the attribute "booleanAction" with the following definitions–
• "not" – Returns the inverse Boolean value of the expression operand.
• "defined" – Returns "true" if the variable is in the state table and is not asserted to "noValue" value.
element.title: Process data module - Logic engine

element.name: booleanOperator
element.definition:
The element <booleanOperator> is an operator between two Boolean expressions in the Process DM that when evaluated returns the value "true" or "false". The operator evaluates the two element <expression>s based on the attribute "booleanOperation" with following definitions–
• "and" – Returns "true" if both Boolean expressions are "true" otherwise returns "false".
• "equal" – Returns "true" if both expressions are identical otherwise returns "false".
• "exclusiveOr" – Returns "true" if one and only one Boolean expression is "true" otherwise returns "false".
• "notEqual" – Returns "true" if the two expressions are different otherwise returns "false".
• "or" – Returns "true" if at least one expression is "true" otherwise returns "false".
element.title: Process data module - Logic engine

element.name: booleanValue
element.definition: The element <booleanValue> is an expression operand that is set to the data type Boolean.
element.title: Process data module - Logic engine

element.name: boundary
element.definition: The element <boundary> describes a boundary, which can be expressed textually or with specific measures (eg, rib or frame).
element.title: Boundary

element.name: boundaryFrom
element.definition:
The elements <boundaryFrom> and <boundaryTo> are used to define the boundaries of a zone, respectively the start and the end boundaries.
A zone can have several "from" boundaries and several "to" boundaries.
element.title: Zone boundaries

element.name: boundaryTo
element.definition:
The elements <boundaryFrom> and <boundaryTo> are used to define the boundaries of a zone, respectively the start and the end boundaries.
A zone can have several "from" boundaries and several "to" boundaries.
element.title: Zone boundaries

element.name: brAction
element.definition: The element <brAction> defines an action that is to be fulfilled to define a complete set of business rules decisions for the BRDP. The information provided under the element <brAction> identifies the owner of the action and if it is complete or not. If one or more child elements <brDecisionRef> are defined, then the element <brAudit> refers both to the BRDP it applies to and to one or more of its business rules decisions. If the <brDecisionRef> is missing, then <brAction> is attributed only to the BRDP defined under the <brPara>. Multiple elements <brAction> within <brPara> make it possible to capture actions not only per BRDP but also per business rules decision. Recording of one <brAction> for a group of business rules decisions defined within the same <brPara> is allowed.
element.title: Business rule action

element.name: brAudit
element.definition: The element <brAudit> captures the audit status on the BRDP and/or business rules decisions defined for it, including possible actions to be accomplished in order to come to a satisfying decision and the status with required date of completion. If one or more child elements <brDecisionRef> are defined, then the element <brAudit> refers both to the BRDP it applies to and to one or more of its business rules decisions. If the <brDecisionRef> is missing, then <brAudit> is attributed only to the BRDP defined under the <brPara>. Multiple elements <brAudit> within a <brPara> make it possible to capture status not only per BRDP but also per business rules decision. Recording of one <brAudit> for a group of business rules decisions defined within the same <brPara> is allowed.
element.title: Business rule audit

element.name: brCategory
element.definition: The element <brCategory> indicates the relationship of the BRPD to a certain business rules category. For example, a decision on "model identifiers" belongs to the business rules category 2, "Product business rules". Refer to Chap 2.5.1.
element.title: Business rule category

element.name: brCategoryDescription
element.definition: The element <brCategoryDescription> provides a description of the related business rules category. Refer to Chap 2.5.1. A project or organization can add any information to assist with understanding and relating to the business rules category or categories.
element.title: Business rule category description

element.name: brCategoryGroup
element.definition: The element <brCategoryGroup> indicates the whole group of business rules categories to which the BRDP relates. Refer to Chap 2.5.1.
element.title: Business rule category group

element.name: brCurrentStatus
element.definition: The element <brCurrentStatus> gives the status of the BRDP and/or related business rules decisions. The business rules data module Schema defines only one <brCurrentStatus> for each element <brAudit>. Therefore, if the <brAudit> does not contain a reference to business rules decisions, then the business rules status also refers to the BRDP as a whole. If there is an element <brAudit> for each business rules decision (or separate group of business rules decisions), then the status also describes a separate business rules decision (or a separate group of business rules decisions).
element.title: Business rule current status

element.name: brDecision
element.definition: The element <brDecision> contains a single decision applicable to the project or organization. A decision is not limited to authoring or publishing, but can contain anything related to the organization or project. These decisions can be included in whole or in part and addressed throughout the lifecycle of the product.
element.title: Business rule decision

element.name: brDecisionExplanation
element.definition: The element <brDecisionExplanation> provides additional information for a business rules definition which was not defined by the element <brDecisionText>. This can be information taken from the specification or any other information formulated by the project or organization. For example, these could be markup examples, extracts from the specification or data/documents from other projects or organizations. The purpose of this added explanation is to enhance the reader’s understanding of the decision made for a BRDP.
element.title: Business rules decision explanation

element.name: brDecisionPending
element.definition: The empty element <brDecisionPending> indicates that a business rule decision, or its part on a BRDP, has not been determined and is "to be defined".
element.title: Business rule decision pending

element.name: brDecisionPointContent
element.definition: The element <brDecisionPointContent> provides information for a BRDP. It includes the BRDP title and definition, BRDP values and their meaning, plus explanation of the BRDP of interest.
element.title: Business rule decision point content

element.name: brDecisionPointExplanation
element.definition: The element <brDecisionPointExplanation> provides additional information on a BRDP, to that provided in the element <brDecisionPointText>. This can be information taken from the specification or any other information required by the project or organization. For example, this could be questions arising when making a decision for a BRDP in a specific context of the project and/or organization.
element.title: Business rule decision point explanation

element.name: brDecisionPointText
element.definition: The element <brDecisionPointText> defines a BRDP for the business rule. For all business rule decision points that have unique identifiers starting with "BRDP-S1-", the text captured in <brDecisionPointText> must be the text defined by this specification. Refer to Chap 2.5.3. The only extension allowed for these BRDP is the reference to the place in the specification where the BRDP is defined. Any explanatory information is to be captured in <brDecisionPointExplanation>. Refer to Para .The text of a project/organizational specific BRDP must be defined specifically for the needs of the project and/or organization. Refer to Chap 2.5.3.
element.title: Business rule decision point text

element.name: brDecisionPointValue
element.definition: The element <brDecisionPointValue> provides information on separate values for the BRDP.
element.title: Business rule decision point value

element.name: brDecisionPointValueGroup
element.definition: The element <brDecisionPointValueGroup> specifies what the BRDP is about. For example, business rules decision points can be about the choice of available values, or about allowing the use of a concept, an element, or an attribute in a certain context. It can also be used to define new values, etc. S1000D provides BRDP values for BRDP defined in the specification and captured inside the business rules template data module. Refer to Chap 2.5.3 and Para . The information mapped in the business rules template data module is based on S1000D text, Schemas and the default BREX.
element.title: Business rule decision point value group

element.name: brDecisionRef
element.definition: The element <brDecisionRef> defines a reference of a business rule decision or another element to a business rule decision. The direct reference from a business rule decision to itself is not allowed.
element.title: Business rule decision reference

element.name: brDecisionText
element.definition: The element <brDecisionText> defines the exact wording of a business rule decision taken for a BRDP.
element.title: Business rule decision text

element.name: brDecisionValue
element.definition: The element <brDecisionValue> defines the type and, where required, the content of a business rules decision within the range and definitions of BRDP values as a recorded decision taken for a BRDP. Refer to Para .
element.title: Business rule decision value

element.name: brDecisionValueGroup
element.definition: The element <brDecisionValueGroup> contains the allowed value, if any. The allowed values can be defined by the project (eg, CAGE code), or the values can be selected from the defined values in the business rule decision point value group (element <brDecisionPointValueGroup>) (eg, language). Refer to Para .
element.title: Business rule decision value group

element.name: brDoc
element.definition: The element <brDoc> has a similar structure to the main content of descriptive data modules with the exception of warningAndCautionElemGroup, which is not included in the Business rules document data module. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.2. There are additional elements and attributes provided in the element <brLevelledPara> for recording business rules information.
element.title: Business rules document

element.name: brex
element.definition: The element <brex> contains all business rules specified by a BREX data module.
element.title: Exchange business rules

element.name: brexDmRef
element.definition: The element <brexDmRef> contains a pointer to the business rules data module (BREX data module) that applies to the data module. For a detailed description of the BREX module and related aspects, refer to Chap 4.10.
element.title: Business rules reference

element.name: brLevelledPara
element.definition:
The element <brLevelledPara> is the equivalent of a headed paragraph and can also contain subparagraphs. Similar to the element <levelledPara> in a descriptive data module, the element <brLevelledPara> can have one or more occurrences of the element <brLevelledPara> nested within it. The depth of this structure is unlimited, however, it is recommended to not exceed five levels of depth.
When a <brLevelledPara> contains a nested <brLevelledPara>, it is recommended for two or more of this element be provided at the same level, otherwise standard heading rules can be violated for the project. Rules and recommendations for <levelledPara> in descriptive data modules apply here. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.2.
element.title: Business rule levelled paragraph

element.name: brPara
element.definition:
The element <brPara> is a child of element <brLevelledPara> in the descriptive structure of a business rules data module. It contains information about:
This includes possible open actions, as well as the closing date when the decisions must be, or were, made.
element.title: Business rule paragraph

element.name: brRelatedTo
element.definition: The element <brRelatedTo> indicates where in the source document the definition of the BRDP can be found and how the BRDP relates to decisions by the project or organization with respect to:
element.title: Business rule decision point relationship

element.name: brRequiredDate
element.definition: The element <brRequiredDate> indicates the date when the business rules decision must be taken and/or the date that the BRDP must be addressed. The business rules data module Schema defines only one <brCurrentStatus> for each element <brAudit>. Therefore, if the element <brAudit> does not contain any reference to business rules decisions, then the required date also refers to the BRDP as a whole. If there is an element <brAudit> for each business rules decision (or separate group of business rules decisions), then the required date also applies to a separate business rules decision (or a separate group of business rules decisions).
element.title: Business rule required date

element.name: brStatusDate
element.definition: The element <brStatusDate> indicates the date when the business rules status was recorded. The business rules data module Schema defines only one <brCurrentStatus> for each element <brAudit>. Therefore, if the element <brAudit> does not contain any reference to business rules decisions, then the status date also refers to the BRDP as a whole. If there is an element <brAudit> for each business rules decision (or separate group of business rules decisions), then the status date also applies to a separate business rules decision (or a separate group of business rules decisions).
element.title: Business rule status date

element.name: building
element.definition: The element <building> contains the building number where the business is located.
element.title: Building identifier

element.name: businessUnit
element.definition: The element <businessUnit> contains information about the business unit, including name, address and contacts.
element.title: Business unit

element.name: businessUnitAddress
element.definition: The element <businessUnitAddress> contains the information required to contact the business unit.
element.title: Business unit address

element.name: businessUnitName
element.definition: The element <businessUnitName> contains a string identifying the name of the business unit.
element.title: Business unit name

element.name: buyerFurnishedEquipFlag
element.definition: The element <buyerFurnishedEquipFlag> indicates whether the customer furnishes the part or not, that is, it enables the data user to identify a buyer furnished equipment (BFE) part. As this value depends on the customer, the attribute applicRefId can be used to specify for which customer(s) the information is valid. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.3.
element.title: Buyer furnished equipment flag

element.name: calibrationMarker
element.definition: The element <calibrationMarker> identifies items that require calibration. The content of element must be "1" if an item requires calibration.
element.title: Calibration marker

element.name: caption
element.definition:
The element <caption> contains a caption representing a single illuminated warning light, push button, annunciator, or display caption. It can be used inline within text content elements such as the element <para>, or grouped within the element <captionGroup> structure as given in Para .
Each caption can contain lines of text in individual occurrences of the element <captionLine>.
element.title: Caption

element.name: captionBody
element.definition: The element <captionBody> is equivalent to the table element <tbody>. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.1.6.
element.title: Caption body

element.name: captionEntry
element.definition: The element <captionEntry> represents a table cell that contain the captions or caption text and is equivalent to the table element <entry>. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.1.6.
element.title: Caption entry

element.name: captionGroup
element.definition: The element <captionGroup> represents a block of illuminated warning lights, push buttons, annunciators, or display captions. The <captionGroup> structure has been designed to provide some basic control of formatting. By using the element it is possible to create caption groups that are visually similar to the real object.
element.title: Caption group

element.name: captionLine
element.definition: The element <captionLine> represents a single line of text, so its text content should not wrap to second or subsequent lines.
element.title: Caption line

element.name: captionRow
element.definition: The element <captionRow> represents a caption table row and is equivalent to the table element <row>. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.1.6.
element.title: Caption row

element.name: captionText
element.definition: The element <captionText> contains the text to be added to a <captionGroup>.
element.title: Caption text

element.name: case
element.definition: The element <case> contains a case as part of the list of possible cases.
element.title: Case statement

element.name: caseCond
element.definition: The element <caseCond> contains the condition as part of an "if" statement.
element.title: Condition statement

element.name: catalogSeqNumber
element.definition: The element <catalogSeqNumber> contains the catalog sequence number and all related parts information.
element.title: Catalog sequence number

element.name: catalogSeqNumberRef
element.definition: The element <catalogSeqNumberRef> contains a reference to another CSN. For non-S2000M projects the attributes systemCode, subSystemCode, subsubSystemCode and assyCode are used to refer to the SNS of the reference target. For non-S2000M projects, the attributes initialProvisioningProjectNumber and responsiblePartnerCompanyCode must not be used.
element.title: Refer to CSN

element.name: categoryOneContainerLocation
element.definition: The element <categoryOneContainerLocation> contains the location at which the data record of an item’s category 1 container is held.
element.title: Category 1 container location

element.name: caution
element.definition:
The element <caution> contains hazard information that can cause damage to the Product.
Cautions in data modules must always precede the text where the hazard arises and must always precede notes.
Cautions of a general nature, however, which are applicable throughout an instruction, can precede the instruction to save repeating the caution before each of the individual steps of the maintenance task where the hazard arises. Refer to Para .
The default heading CAUTION must begin the caution followed by symbols, if any. Refer to Chap 6.2.2 for examples.
element.title: Cautions

element.name: cautionIdent
element.definition: The element <cautionIdent> uniquely identifies the caution thru its attribute cautionIdentNumber. This identifier must be used to refer from the data modules where the caution is externalized (element <cautionRef>) to the externalized caution in the cautions CIR data module.
element.title: Caution identifier

element.name: cautionRef
element.definition: The element <cautionRef> contains the reference to a caution in a cautions CIR data module. It is used for externalizing cautions. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.11.14.
element.title: References to externalized cautions

element.name: cautionRepository
element.definition: The element <cautionRepository> contains a collection of cautions used in the data modules.
element.title: Cautions repository

element.name: cautionSpec
element.definition: The element <cautionSpec> represents one caution.
element.title: Caution specification

element.name: challenge
element.definition: The element <challenge> contains the crew/operator challenge.
element.title: Challenge

element.name: challengeAndResponse
element.definition: The element <challengeAndResponse> contains the crew/operator challenge and response.
element.title: Crew/operator challenge and response

element.name: changeAuthority
element.definition:
The element <changeAuthority> represents one impacting condition. It corresponds to the S2000M definition of change authority number (CAN).
Either the content of element <changeAuthority> or its attributes can be used to store the CAN information.
element.title: Change authority

element.name: changeAuthorityData
element.definition: The element <changeAuthorityData> contains conditions impacting a part or a wire. These impacting conditions indicate why it is allowed to install this part or wire. Generally, this indication consists of a condition (eg, design office modification, Service bulletin, or customer originated change).
element.title: Change authority data

element.name: changeAuthorityDataGroup
element.definition: The element <changeAuthorityDataGroup> contains a list of conditions impacting the wire.
element.title: Change authority data group

element.name: changeInline
element.definition: The element <changeInline> must be used for changed data modules when changing parts of text within elements. The element <changeInline> must not be used to indicate that a complete element has been added, modified or deleted. Refer to default BREX rule BREX-S1-00040.
element.title: Changing text inline

element.name: checkList
element.definition: The element <checkList> contains the checklist information. For each category identified, an organization or project can establish business rules for which elements to use and/or how to markup checklists for each category type.
element.title: Checklist and maintenance planning information

element.name: checkListInfo
element.definition: The element <checkListInfo> contains information necessary to group the items that create the checklist data.
element.title: Checklist information

element.name: checkListInterval
element.definition: used to capture the individual intervals corresponding to the checklist
element.title: checklist

element.name: checkListIntervals
element.definition: The element <checkListIntervals> contains the various maintenance intervals used in an inspection or checklist. These intervals can be used to capture information for use with maintenance interval columns. For example, a table might have a column for 500 hours and a column for 1000 hours. These columns would then have a check mark in the appropriate task row to indicate when the maintenance should be performed.
element.title: Checklist intervals

element.name: checkListItem
element.definition: The element contains a number of elements used to capture individual items specific for an inspection or maintenance check. The element <checkListItem> presents an optional item number, followed by an optional threshold.
element.title: Checklist item

element.name: checkListItems
element.definition: The element <checkListItems> contains one or more individual checklist items (<checkListItem>).
element.title: Checklist items

element.name: checkListPara
element.definition: The element <checkListPara> contains the statement necessary to indicate which check should be performed, when only one statement is needed.
element.title: Checklist paragraph

element.name: checkListProcedure
element.definition: The element <checkListProcedure> contains a brief description of each check to be performed as well as any information required to accomplish a check or service. This element contains either paragraph text or a series of checklist steps.
element.title: Checklist procedure

element.name: checkListStep
element.definition: The element <checkListStep> contains the statements
element.title: Checklist step

element.name: choice
element.definition: The element <choice> provides the internal link to the next action related to a selection from a choice of answers.
element.title: Choice

element.name: circuitBreaker
element.definition:
The element <circuitBreaker> contains information that describes the circuit breaker and provides further information related to the circuit breaker based on the Product configuration.
Configuration consistency between the various alternate circuit breakers must be ensured. This means that for a given Product configuration and under given operational conditions, only one alternate circuit breaker is applicable at one time for a given circuit breaker.
element.title: Alternate circuit breaker

element.name: circuitBreakerAlts
element.definition: The element <circuitBreakerAlts> is a group of circuit breaker alternates.
element.title: Circuit breaker alternate group

element.name: circuitBreakerClass
element.definition: The element <circuitBreakerClass> indicates whether the circuit breaker is electronic or electro-mechanic.
element.title: Circuit breaker class

element.name: circuitBreakerDescr
element.definition: The element <circuitBreakerDescr> contains the information on individual circuit breakers.
element.title: Circuit breaker description

element.name: circuitBreakerDescrGroup
element.definition: The element <circuitBreakerDescrGroup> contains the circuit breaker lists information.
element.title: Circuit breaker description group

element.name: circuitBreakerDescrSubGroup
element.definition: The element <circuitBreakerDescrSubGroup> contains one or more sub circuit breaker lists.
element.title: Circuit breaker description subgroup

element.name: circuitBreakerIdent
element.definition:
The element <circuitBreakerIdent> semantically identifies the circuit breaker as a result of the constraints on its attributes. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.
The circuit breaker can be used to uniquely identify a device that is used to break an electrical circuit or to render an electrical function inactive.
element.title: Circuit breaker identifier

element.name: circuitBreakerLocation
element.definition: The element <circuitBreakerLocation> contains the textual description of the circuit breaker location in the circuit breaker panel.
element.title: Circuit breaker location

element.name: circuitBreakerRef
element.definition: The element <circuitBreakerRef> contains the details about the circuit breaker (eg, number, circuit breaker action and circuit breaker type).
element.title: Circuit breaker

element.name: circuitBreakerRefGroup
element.definition: The element <circuitBreakerRefGroup> provides relationships and the types of relationships with other circuit breakers.
element.title: References to other circuit breakers

element.name: circuitBreakerRepository
element.definition: The element <circuitBreakerRepository> provides a list of circuit breakers and their associated properties.
element.title: Circuit breaker repository

element.name: circuitBreakerSpec
element.definition:
The element <circuitBreakerSpec> contains the associated properties of one circuit breaker.
Some properties of the circuit breaker can vary from one product instance to another. Such properties are managed under the circuit breaker alternates (element <circuitBreakerAlts>), which can contain a reference to an applicability annotation. Refer to Chap 4.13.3 for details on the alternates concept.
For some properties appearing at both levels (eg, <name> or <shortName>), the following rule must be applied: if the property is fulfilled in the alternate element, it supersedes the generic value stored in the parent element.
element.title: Circuit breaker specification

element.name: circuitIdent
element.definition:
The element <circuitIdent> is used to identify the wire.
For projects that do not use the circuit code of a wire in the identification of the wire itself, the circuit code element <circuitIdent> is additionally available in the wire information element <wireInfo>.
element.title: Circuit code

element.name: city
element.definition: The element <city> contains the name of the city where the business is located.
element.title: City

element.name: classificationAction
element.definition: The element <classificationAction> contains the classification action.
element.title: Classification action

element.name: classificationActionGroup
element.definition: The element <classificationActionGroup> contains the set of classification actions that can be referred to using the attribute derivativeClassificationRefId within the element <businessUnit>.
element.title: Classification action group

element.name: clippingPoint
element.definition: The clipping points of a wire that fasten it to the structure of an aircraft, an equipment, etc, are listed in the <clippingPoint> elements. If used, every clipping point must be populated in exactly one of the <clippingPoint> elements (ie, the clipping point identification contains only one clipping point that fastens the wire to the structure).
element.title: Clipping point information

element.name: closeRqmts
element.definition: The element <closeRqmts> contains any actions that are required after the procedure is complete.
element.title: General

element.name: coaxialCableFlag
element.definition: The coax information element <coaxialCableFlag> identifies coaxial wire material (contacts or cables). It is recommended to identify coaxial contacts by populating this element with the value "Y". If the contact is not a coaxial contact, this element can be populated with the value "N", or not used at all.
element.title: Coaxial information

element.name: colspec
element.definition: The element <colspec> (column specification) is used in the table group (<tgroup>) and can be overridden by the element <colspec> in the table header (<thead>), the table footer (<tfoot>) and the table body (<tbody>). When used in the table header, table footer and table body, the values specified override those that can be present on the table group and these values are in turn overridden by the settings of the same attributes on individual rows and entries.
element.title: Column specification

element.name: comment
element.definition: The comment form consists of two main sections of information: the identification and status section, and the content section.
element.title: Comment form

element.name: commentAddress
element.definition: The element <commentAddress> consists of two blocks of information items used to address the comment object, the comment identification and the supplementary comment address items.
element.title: Comment address

element.name: commentAddressItems
element.definition: The element <commentAddressItems> contains the complementary address items, supporting identification of a comment object.
element.title: Comment address items

element.name: commentCode
element.definition:
The element <commentCode> contains the comment code. Together with the issue information, it gives an identification that is unique within the S1000D domain.
The comment code is comprised of up to 29 alphanumeric characters and is built up as shown in . The minimum length is 17 characters.
YY - YYYYY - XXXX - NNNNN - A (17 characters)
thru
YYYYYYYYYYYYYY - YYYYY - XXXX - NNNNN - A (29 characters)
Table 2 Comment code
element.title: Comment code

element.name: commentContent
element.definition: The element <commentContent> contains the comment and/or the response to a comment and references to attachments.
element.title: Comment form content section - markup requirements

element.name: commentIdent
element.definition: The element <commentIdent> contains the unique identity of a comment object.
element.title: Comment identification

element.name: commentOriginator
element.definition: The element <commentOriginator> contains information about the originator of the comment.
element.title: Originator

element.name: commentPriority
element.definition: The element <commentPriority> contains the priority code of the comment.
element.title: Comment priority

element.name: commentRef
element.definition: The element <commentRef> contains a reference to a comment form included in the data management list.
element.title: Comment form reference

element.name: commentRefIdent
element.definition: The element <commentRefIdent> is used to provide the identity of a comment form to which a comment form reference is pointing.
element.title: Comment form reference identity

element.name: commentRefs
element.definition: The element <commentRefs> contains the reference to the information the comment is referring to. References are allowed to publication modules (including the entire publication set via list of applicable publications), data modules, data management lists or data dispatch notes. For each comment raised, there must be at least one reference. Comments to the comments are not permitted (use the element <noReferences> when a response to a comment is submitted).
element.title: Comment references

element.name: commentResponse
element.definition: The element <commentResponse> contains the type code of response to the comment.
element.title: Comment response

element.name: commentStatus
element.definition: The element <commentStatus> contains the status information for the comment form.
element.title: Comment status

element.name: commentTitle
element.definition: the title of the comment, given as a simple text string.
element.title: title of the comment

element.name: commonInfo
element.definition: The element <commonInfo> contains descriptive information that applies to an entire data module.
element.title: Definition

element.name: commonInfoDescrPara
element.definition: The element <commonInfoDescrPara> is used to contain text information.
element.title: Descriptive paragraph definition

element.name: commonInfoDescrParaAlts
element.definition: The element <commonInfoDescrParaAlts> is an alternates group that provides the capability to group several alternate solutions of a descriptive text (<commonInfoDescrPara>). Refer to Chap 4.13.3 for information related to the use of alternates group in data module content.
element.title: Descriptive paragraph alternates group definition

element.name: commonRepository
element.definition: The element <commonRepository> contains one type of CIR data module from the list of CIR data module types. For each CIR type, it is possible to add an introductory text (element <commonInfo>) and to store illustrations (element <figure> and/or <figureAlts>) or multimedia objects (elements <multimedia> and/or <multimediaAlts>) associated to the CIR entries.
element.title: Common information repository

element.name: complianceCategory
element.definition: The element <complianceCategory> indicates whether the alternatives listed within the supply requirement are strictly limited to the ones listed or not, and to what extent the list of alternatives can be extended.
element.title: Compliance category

element.name: componentAssy
element.definition: The element <componentAssy> contains the identification of the item (component assembly) by name (a basic name and a noun word or phrase modifier, for example, transformer). Parts that are not subject to maintenance must not be listed.
element.title: Component assembly

element.name: componentAssyGroup
element.definition: The element <componentAssyGroup> contains a group of subassemblies when there is a need to divide the item (component assembly).
element.title: Component assembly group

element.name: cond
element.definition: The element <cond> declares a single condition of the Product.
element.title: Condition

element.name: condCrossRefTable
element.definition: The element <condCrossRefTable> is used to provide the content of the CCT data module, which is divided into three components:
element.title: Conditions cross-reference table

element.name: condCrossRefTableRef
element.definition: The element <condCrossRefTableRef> provides a reference to the CCT data module for this project.
element.title: CCT reference

element.name: condDescr
element.definition: The element <condDescr> specifies a condition that is not defined in the CCT.
element.title: Condition description

element.name: condIncorporation
element.definition: The element <condIncorporation> represents a technical condition that has or is planned to be incorporated into the technical data. It can reference a declared condition (element <cond>) and consists of a list of documents affected by the technical condition.
element.title: Condition incorporation

element.name: condList
element.definition: The element <condList> contains a list of specific conditions which have an effect on the applicability of technical data.
element.title: Conditions list

element.name: condRef
element.definition: The element <condRef> identifies a single condition that is defined in the CCT condition list using the element <cond> or the element <externalCond>.
element.title: Condition reference

element.name: condRefGroup
element.definition: The element <condRefGroup> refers to other conditions linked to the described conditions. It is possible to refer to a defined condition using the element <condRef> or to another condition using the element <condDescr>.
element.title: Group of referenced conditions

element.name: condType
element.definition: The element <condType> defines a single condition type of the Product.
element.title: Condition type

element.name: condTypeList
element.definition: The element <condTypeList> contains a list of common types of conditions which will be used as a basis for defining specific conditions.
element.title: Condition types list

element.name: conduit
element.definition:
The element <conduit> is contained in the list of conduits element <conduitGroup>. The list of conduits is a child element of the list of the standard parts element <standardPartGroup>.
It contains information concerning the properties of conduits used in the Product's wiring.
element.title: Element <conduit> and child elements

element.name: conduitGroup
element.definition: the list of conduits
element.title: Electrical standard parts

element.name: configAttributes
element.definition: The element <configAttributes> indicates the relationship of the BRDP to configurable attributes (ie, to the attributes defined both in Chap 3.9.6.1 and Chap 3.9.6.2). By filtering BRDP for configurable attributes, it is possible to identify specific context dependent values for each configurable attribute. It is also possible to identify if these attributes can have the same range of values for all elements and for all contexts.
element.title: Relation to configurable attributes

element.name: connectionListClass
element.definition:
The element <connectionListClass> describes special groups of electrical equipment (eg, switches, connectors) in coded form.
It is recommended to use the connection list class element <connectionListClass> as given in .
Table 2 Connection list classes
element.title: Connection list class

element.name: connector
element.definition:
The element <connector> is contained in the connector list element <connectorGroup>. The list of connectors is a child element of the list of standard parts element <standardPartGroup>.
The element <connector> contains information concerning the properties of connectors used in the Product's wiring. It is also used to describe terminal modules, switches and relays.
element.title: Element <connector> and child elements

element.name: connectorAccessories
element.definition:
The element <connectorAccessories> contains the part numbers of accessories for the connector.
More than one accessory of the same kind can be available for a connector (eg, cable clamps with different angular positions). In this case, all possible accessories can be added to the connector, which results in more than one cable clamp appearing in the list of accessories. Another method in this case is to use different connector containers for the same connector, listing only one accessory of the same kind. This second method can generate a considerable amount of connector data. Therefore it is recommended to list all available accessories in one data set.
element.title: List of accessories

element.name: connectorGroup
element.definition: the list of connectors
element.title: Electrical standard parts

element.name: contact
element.definition: The element <contact> describes the contact identification by its attributes. Normally, wires are connected to contacts with a given name. If not, the attribute contactIdent has no value for those contacts where at least one of the other attributes is used. If none of the attributes is used, the element <contact> must not be used at all.
element.title: Contact identification

element.name: contactCount
element.definition: The element <contactCount> contains the number of contacts of the connector.
element.title: Contact count

element.name: contactDescr
element.definition: The element <contactDescr> contains a list of contact descriptions.
element.title: Contact description

element.name: contactDescrGroup
element.definition: The element <contactDescrGroup> contains a list of contact descriptions.
element.title: List of contact descriptions

element.name: contactDiameter
element.definition: The element <contactDiameter> describes the diameter of a contact.
element.title: Contact diameter

element.name: contactIdentification
element.definition: The element <contactIdentification> contains the contact identification of a functional item.
element.title: Contact identification

element.name: contactInfo
element.definition: The element <contactInfo> contains child elements that identify the contact to which the wire end is connected.
element.title: Contact information

element.name: contactOrderValue
element.definition:
The element <contactOrderValue> is used to order the wire ends, connected to equipment with internal logic, in addition to the termination module, block and shunt grouping information.
All wires, which are not connected to base contacts of switches and relays, must get values higher than the values of base contacts. In this case, the base contact (supply) will be shown before the different positions of the switch/relay in the graphical presentation of the wires connected to this equipment.
When more than one wire is connected to an electrical potential, all these wires must have the same content in the element <contactOrderValue>.
element.title: Contact order information

element.name: contactPerson
element.definition: The element <contactPerson> contains information related to individual contacts of a business unit.
element.title: Contact persons

element.name: contactPersonAddress
element.definition: The element <contactPersonAddress> contains the information required to contact the contact person.
element.title: Contact person address

element.name: contactSize
element.definition: The element <contactSize> contains size information about a distribution part, normally for a contact.
element.title: Contact size

element.name: container
element.definition: The element <container> contains the list of alternate data modules, which are in the container.
element.title: Container

element.name: content
element.definition: The element <content> contains the content section of data module.
element.title: Content

element.name: contentDescription
element.definition: The element <contentDescription> supports the insertion of annotations concerning the content of the SCO content data module.
element.title: Content description

element.name: contextAndIsolationInfo
element.definition: The element <contextAndIsolationInfo> contains the description of the context in which the fault is observed and information needed to isolate the faulty item.
element.title: Context and isolation information

element.name: contextRules
element.definition:
The element <contextRules> contains rules that can be related to one or more Schema contexts (ie, rules regarding the use of elements in various Schemas).
The element can be repeated to give rules that are specific to different Schemas, where the Schema concerned is identified by the attribute rulesContext. If the attribute is not specified the contained rules will commonly apply to all objects, regardless of Schema.
Rules given for a specific Schema override rules applicable to all Schema contexts. Rules specific to a Schema must be true restrictions to commonly applicable rules.
element.title: Rules related to context

element.name: contributor
element.definition:
An entity responsible for making contributions to the content of the resource. Examples of a contributor include a person, an organization, or a service. Typically, the name of a contributor is used to indicate the entity.
S1000D maps the originator to this Dublin core element, ie, the value of attribute enterpriseCode if given, otherwise the content of element <enterpriseName>).
element.title: Dublin core elements

element.name: controlIndicatorDescr
element.definition: The element <controlIndicatorDescr> contains the information specific to a control or indicator.
element.title: Control or indicator description

element.name: controlIndicatorFunction
element.definition: The element <controlIndicatorFunction> contains one or more brief descriptions of the control or indicator function.
element.title: Control or indicator function

element.name: controlIndicatorGroup
element.definition: The element <controlIndicatorGroup> contains a unique identification of controls and indicators, each grouped by equipment, assembly, or control panel.
element.title: Control or indicator group

element.name: controlIndicatorIdent
element.definition: The fragment identifier for a Controls and indicators fragment
element.title: fragment identifier

element.name: controlIndicatorKey
element.definition: The element <controlIndicatorKey> contains the illustration or index key of the control or indicator.
element.title: Control or indicator key

element.name: controlIndicatorName
element.definition: The element <controlIndicatorName> contains the name of the control or indicator.
element.title: Control or indicator name

element.name: controlIndicatorRef
element.definition: The element <controlIndicatorRef> contains the number and name of the controls or indicators and the reference to the control and indicator repository data module.
element.title: Controls and indicators

element.name: controlIndicatorRepository
element.definition: The element <controlIndicatorRepository> contains control and indicator information.
element.title: Controls and indicators repository

element.name: controlIndicatorSpec
element.definition: The element <controlIndicatorSpec> contains control and indicator information including illustration or index key, name, and description. This element is the target when a control or indicator is referenced from another data module, for example from a procedural data module, using the element <controlIndicatorRef>. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.1.10.
element.title: Control and indicator information

element.name: copyright
element.definition:
The element <copyright> contains the copyright information.
There are two methods for populating copyright information for a particular data module. These are:
Both methods are permitted.
element.title: Copyright

element.name: copyrightPara
element.definition: The element <copyrightPara> contains the copyright statement itself or provides a cross-reference to another data module that contains the statement.
element.title: Copyright paragraph

element.name: correlatedFault
element.definition: The element <correlatedFault> contains the information on correlated faults.
element.title: Correlated faults

element.name: correlatedFaultAlts
element.definition: The element <correlatedFaultAlts> is an alternates group that provides the capability to group several alternate solutions of a correlated fault. Refer to Chap 4.13.3 for information related to the use of alternates group in data module content.
element.title: Correlated faults alternates group

element.name: country
element.definition: The element <country> contains the International Standards Organization (ISO) country code where the business is located.
element.title: Country Code

element.name: creator
element.definition:
An entity primarily responsible for making the content of the resource. Examples of a creator include a person, an organization, or a service. Typically, the name of a creator is used to indicate the entity.
S1000D maps the content of element <originator> to this Dublin core element for data modules and the element <responsiblePartnerCompany> for publication modules and SCORM content package modules.
element.title: Dublin core elements

element.name: crew
element.definition: The element <crew> is the container for all crew/operator content (excluding references).
element.title: Crew

element.name: crewDrill
element.definition: The element <crewDrill> contains a crew/operator drill.
element.title: Drill

element.name: crewDrillStep
element.definition: The element <crewDrillStep> contains a step in a crew/operator data module.
element.title: Crew drill step

element.name: crewMember
element.definition: The element <crewMember> contains the identification of a crew/operator member.
element.title: Crew member

element.name: crewMemberGroup
element.definition: The element <crewMemberGroup> contains the identification of the crew/operator responsible for carrying out an action.
element.title: Crew

element.name: crewProcedureName
element.definition: The element <crewProcedureName> contains the crew/operator procedure.
element.title: Crew/operator procedure

element.name: crewRefCard
element.definition: The element <crewRefCard> contains a crew/operator reference card information for drills.
element.title: Reference card

element.name: customerStockNumber
element.definition: The element <customerStockNumber> contains the stock number assigned by the customer to identify and control the part in its inventory and control system. As this number depends on the customer, the attribute applicRefId and the referenced element <applic> must both be populated in accordance with Chap 3.9.5.3.
element.title: Customer stock number

element.name: dataConds
element.definition: The element <dataConds> contains any information on any special conditions that can apply to the data module. For example, the security classification of a data module can be required to be changed when aggregated with other data modules with similar conditions.
element.title: Data conditions

element.name: dataDestruction
element.definition: The element <dataDestruction> contains any destruction instructions that are specific to the data module and that must be applied.
element.title: Data destruction

element.name: dataDisclosure
element.definition: The element <dataDisclosure> contains any special instructions with regard to the dissemination and disclose of the data module.
element.title: Data disclosure

element.name: dataDistribution
element.definition: The element <dataDistribution> contains the instructions for distribution of a data module.
element.title: Data distribution

element.name: dataHandling
element.definition: The element <dataHandling> contains any handling instructions, including storage, that are specific to the data module.
element.title: Data handling

element.name: dataRestrictions
element.definition: The element <dataRestrictions> contains the instructions and information, applicable to the complete data module or publication module, that relate to the use, storage and handling. This element can contain applicability information.
element.title: Data restrictions

element.name: dataUpdateFile
element.definition: The element <dataUpdateFile> contains the complete data update file. The data update file is not a data module so it has its own definition. But like data modules, the data update file is split into two parts:
element.title: Data update file

element.name: date
element.definition:
A date associated with an event in the life cycle of the resource. Typically, <dc:date> will be associated with the creation or availability of the resource. Recommended best practice for encoding the date value is defined in a profile of International Standards Organization (ISO) 8601 and follows the YYYY-MM-DD format.
S1000D maps the data module or publication module or SCORM content package module issue date to this Dublin core element, (ie values of attribute year, attribute month and attribute day of element <issueDate>, separated by hyphens as indicated below).
element.title: Dublin core elements

element.name: ddn
element.definition:
An S1000D file based transfer package consists of one data dispatch note text file, marked up with XML, and at least one file out of the data categories given in Chap 4.8.
The files can be transferred in any order, but it is recommended that the data dispatch note is the first data file in the sequence for serial media. A directory structure will not be used.
element.title: File based transfer

element.name: ddnAddress
element.definition: to be found in DDN schema
element.title: Data dispatch note

element.name: ddnAddressItems
element.definition: to be found in DDN schema
element.title: Data dispatch note

element.name: ddnCode
element.definition: This element <ddnCode> contains the code identifying the referred data dispatch note.
element.title: Data dispatch note identification code

element.name: ddnContent
element.definition: to be found in DDN schema
element.title: Data dispatch note

element.name: ddnIdent
element.definition: to be found in DDN schema
element.title: mediaIdent

element.name: ddnRef
element.definition: The element <ddnRef> contains a reference to a data dispatch note to which the comment relates.
element.title: Data dispatch note reference

element.name: ddnRefAddressItems
element.definition: The element <ddnRefAddressItems> contains a references to a data dispatch note to which the comment relates.
element.title: Supplementary addressing information pertinent to a dispatch note

element.name: ddnRefGroup
element.definition: The element <ddnRefGroup> contains one or several references to data dispatch notes to which the comment relates.
element.title: Data dispatch note reference group

element.name: ddnRefIdent
element.definition: The element <ddnRefIdent> contains the unique identity of a referred data dispatch note.
element.title: Data dispatch note reference identity

element.name: ddnStatus
element.definition: to be found in DDN schema
element.title: Data dispatch note

element.name: default
element.definition: The user entry can have a default value that pre-populates the user entry field with the value.
element.title: Default user entry

element.name: definitionList
element.definition:
The element <definitionList> contains a list of terms and definitions. It can contain applicability information, a title and a definition list header. The body of the definition list comprises one or more definition list items, each containing a term and its definition.
It is possible to have more than one paragraph in a definition.
The definition list is presented as a two column table that contains terms and definitions. Examples for use are the definition of terms and legends. Refer to Chap 6.2.2.
element.title: Definition list

element.name: definitionListHeader
element.definition: The element <definitionListHeader> contains the column headers of the definition list. It can contain the term title and the definition title.
element.title: Definition list headers

element.name: definitionListItem
element.definition: The element <definitionListItem> contains a definition list item.
element.title: Definition list item

element.name: definitionTitle
element.definition: The element <definitionTitle> contains the column title for the definitions.
element.title: Definition title

element.name: deleteObject
element.definition:
The element <deleteObject> represents one data module fragment that has been deleted since a previous issue of the data module. When retrieving the new issue of the data module, this fragment must be deleted. For deleted data module fragments, the data update file indicates the identifier of the object to delete only. For example, the data update file of the part CIR data module, the element <deleteObject> will contain the element <partIdent> indicating that the element <partSpec> containing this <partIdent> must be deleted.
Only the objects that are allowed into the data module can be used in a data update file. For example the data update file related to the part CIR data module must delete <partSpec> elements only.
element.title: Object to delete

element.name: deleteObjectGroup
element.definition: The element <deleteObjectGroup> contains all the data module fragments that have been deleted since a previous issue of the data module. When retrieving the new issue of the data module, these fragments must be deleted.
element.title: Group of objects to delete

element.name: deliveryList
element.definition: to be found in DDN schema. wrapper element to make structure more easily understood
element.title: Data dispatch note

element.name: deliveryListItem
element.definition: The element <deliveryListItem> specifies one dispatched object in the transfer package.
element.title: Data dispatch note

element.name: department
element.definition: The element <department> contains the identification of the business department.
element.title: Department

element.name: dependency
element.definition: The element <dependency> identifies a condition that is dependent on the existence of another condition.
element.title: Dependency

element.name: derivativeClassification
element.definition: The element <derivativeClassification> contains all derivative classification actions taken (eg, source material, date of action, action type) to the information in the complete publication.
element.title: Derivative classification

element.name: derivativeSource
element.definition: The element <derivativeSource> contains a string that identifies the material from which the classification is derived.
element.title: Derivative source

element.name: descr
element.definition: The element <descr> is used to contain further clarification of the meaning.
element.title: Description

element.name: descrAccessDoorOrPanel
element.definition: The element <descrAccessDoorOrPanel> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the access door or panel child element <accessDoorOrPanel> of the element <installationInfo>.
element.title: Access door or panel information description

element.name: descrAccessory
