element.definition: The element <descrAccessory> contains all relevant elements to describe the accessory data elements of the project.
element.title: Accessory data/field descriptions

element.name: descrAccessoryPartNumber
element.definition: The element <descrAccessoryPartNumber> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the accessory part number child element <partNumber> of the list of accessories element <connectorAccessories>.
element.title: Accessory part number description

element.name: descrAltPartNumber
element.definition: The element <descrAltPartNumber> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the part number child element <partNumber> of the element <altIdent> within the list of alternative identifications.
element.title: Alternative part number description

element.name: descrAmperage
element.definition: The element <descrAmperage> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the amperage information element <amperage>.
element.title: Amperage information description

element.name: descrAssy
element.definition: The element <descrAssy> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the assembly information child element <assy> of the assembly instructions element <assyInstruction>.
element.title: Assembly information description

element.name: descrAttenuation
element.definition: The element <descrAttenuation> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the attenuation information child element <attenuation> of the frequency and attenuation element <frequencyAttenuation>.
element.title: Attenuation information description

element.name: descrBlockGroupingValue
element.definition: The element <descrBlockGroupingValue> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the block grouping information child element <blockGroupingValue> of the contact description element <contactDescr>.
element.title: Block grouping information description

element.name: descrCircuitIdent
element.definition: The element <descrCircuitIdent> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the circuit code child element <circuitIdent> of the element <wireIdent>.
element.title: Circuit code description

element.name: descrCoaxialCableFlag
element.definition: The element <descrCoaxialCableFlag> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the coaxial information element <coaxialCableFlag>.
element.title: Coaxial information description

element.name: descrConduit
element.definition: The element <descrConduit> contains all relevant elements to describe the conduit data elements of the project.
element.title: Conduit data/field descriptions

element.name: descrConnectionListClass
element.definition: The element <descrConnectionListClass> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the connection list class element <connectionListClass>.
element.title: Connection list class description

element.name: descrConnector
element.definition: The element <descrConnector> contains all relevant elements to describe the connector data elements of the project.
element.title: Connector data/field descriptions

element.name: descrContact
element.definition: The element <descrContact> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the contact information element <contact>.
element.title: Contact information description

element.name: descrContactCount
element.definition: The element <descrContactCount> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the contact count element <contactCount>.
element.title: Contact count description

element.name: descrContactDiameter
element.definition: The element <descrContactDiameter> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the contact diameter element <contactDiameter>.
element.title: Contact diameter description

element.name: descrContactSize
element.definition: The element <descrContactSize> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the contact size element <contactSize>.
element.title: Contact size description

element.name: descrCrew
element.definition: The element <descrCrew> contains descriptive information for use by crew/operators.
element.title: Descriptive information for crew/operators

element.name: descrDistributionPart
element.definition: The element <descrDistributionPart> contains all relevant elements to describe the distribution part data elements of the project.
element.title: Distribution part data/field descriptions

element.name: descrElectricalContact
element.definition: The element <descrElectricalContact> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the electrical connection contact child element <contact> of the electrical equipment connection element <electricalEquipConnection>.
element.title: Electrical connection contact description

element.name: descrElectricalEquip
element.definition: The element <descrElectricalEquip> contains all relevant elements to describe the electrical equipment data elements of the project.

element.name: descrElectricalStateDescr
element.definition: The element <descrElectricalStateDescr> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the electrical state description child element <electricalStateDescr> of the electrical equipment state information element <electricalEquipState>.

element.definition: The element <descrEmcCode> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the EMC code element <emcCode>.

element.definition: The element <descrEquipDescrRef> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the electrical equipment description reference element <equipDescrRef>.

element.name: descrEquipName
element.definition: The element <descrEquipName> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the electrical equipment name element <equipName>.
element.title: Electrical equipment name description

element.name: descrFeedThru
element.definition: The element <descrFeedThru> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the functional item reference element <functionalItemRef> and the attribute holeIdent of the feed-thru information element <feedThru>.

element.name: descrFinishingInfo
element.definition: The element <descrFinishingInfo> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the finishing information element <finishingInfo>.

element.name: descrForLocation
element.definition: The element <descrForLocation> contains a description for the location of the item which complements the description of the item stored in the element <descrForPart>.
element.title: Description for location

element.name: descrForPart
element.definition: The element <descrForPart> contains a detailed description for a part. It corresponds to the S2000M data element DFP and must be the identifying noun of the item, followed by appropriate adjective and further details, if necessary.
element.title: Description for part

element.name: descrFrequency
element.definition: The element <descrFrequency> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the frequency information child element <frequency> of the frequency and attenuation element <frequencyAttenuation>.
element.title: Frequency information description

element.name: descrFunctionalDescrRef
element.definition: The element <descrFunctionalDescrRef> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the functional description reference element <functionalDescrRef>.
element.title: Functional description reference description

element.name: descrFunctionalItemRef
element.definition: The element <descrFunctionalItemRef> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the functional item reference element <functionalItemRef>. Functional items are also known as reference designators.
element.title: Functional item reference description

element.name: descrGroupCode
element.definition: The element <descrGroupCode> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the group code element <groupCode>.
element.title: Group code description

element.name: descrHarness
element.definition: The element <descrHarness> contains all relevant elements to describe the harness data elements of the project.
element.title: Harness data/field descriptions

element.name: descrHarnessEnvironment
element.definition: The element <descrHarnessEnvironment> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the harness environment element <harnessEnvironment>.
element.title: Harness environment description

element.name: descrHarnessIdent
element.definition: The element <descrHarnessIdent> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the harness identification element <harnessIdent>.

element.name: descrHarnessName
element.definition: The element <descrHarnessName> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the harness name element <harnessName>.

element.name: descrHarnessVariantIdent

element.name: descrHarnessVariantIssue
element.definition: The element <descrHarnessVariantIssue> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the harness variant issue element <harnessVariantIssue>.

element.name: descrIdentSleeve

element.name: descrIllustrationRef
element.definition: The element <descrIllustrationRef> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the illustration reference element <illustrationRef>.

element.name: descrImpedance
element.definition: The element <descrImpedance> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the impedance information child element <impedance> of the frequency characteristics element <frequencyCharacteristics>.

element.name: descrInstallationLocation
element.definition: The element <descrInstallationLocation> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the installation location information child element <installationLocation> of the element <installationInfo>.

element.name: description
element.definition:
The element <description> contains the descriptive information. Descriptive paragraphs can be broken down into subparagraphs, which can be broken down further into sub-subparagraphs and so on.
This element also contains the elements <figure>, <figureAlts>, <multimedia> and <multimediaAlts>.
element.title: Description

element.name: descrLength
element.definition: The element <descrLength> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the length element <length>.
element.title: Length description

element.name: descrManufacturerCode
element.definition: The element <descrManufacturerCode> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the manufacturer code child element <manufacturerCode> of the element <altIdent> within the list of alternative identifications.

element.name: descrMass
element.definition: The element <descrMass> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the mass information element <mass>.
element.title: Mass information description

element.name: descrMaterial
element.definition: The element <descrMaterial> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the material information element <material>.

element.name: descrMaxTemperature

element.definition: The element <descrScreenCount> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the screen count element <screenCount>.

element.name: descrSectionIdent

element.name: descrSpecialTerminal
element.definition: The element <descrSpecialTerminal> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the special terminal information element <specialTerminal>.
element.title: Special terminal information description

element.name: descrStandardPartGroup
element.definition: The element <descrStandardPartGroup> contains child elements, which describe the electrical standard parts data elements.
element.title: Standard parts data/field descriptions

element.name: descrSurfaceProtection
element.definition: The element <descrSurfaceProtection> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the surface protection information element <surfaceProtection>.
element.title: Surface protection information description

element.name: descrSystemBreakdownCode
element.definition: The element <descrSystemBreakdownCode> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the system breakdown code element <systemBreakdownCode>.
element.title: System breakdown code description

element.name: descrTerminationModuleGroupingValue
element.definition: The element <descrTerminationModuleGroupingValue> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the termination module grouping information child element <terminationModuleGroupingValue> of the contact description element <contactDescr>.
element.title: Termination module grouping information description

element.name: descrThermoCoupleMinus
element.definition: The element <descrThermoCoupleMinus> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the thermocouple minus information element <thermoCoupleMinus>.
element.title: Thermocouple minus information description

element.name: descrThermoCouplePlus
element.definition: The element <descrThermoCouplePlus> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the thermocouple plus information element <thermoCouplePlus>.
element.title: Thermocouple plus information description

element.name: descrTransverseLink
element.definition: The element <descrTransverseLink> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the contact identification child element <contact> of the electrical equipment connection element <electricalEquipConnection> within the transverse link element <transverseLink>.
element.title: Transverse link information description

element.name: descrTriaxialCableFlag
element.definition: The element <descrTriaxialCableFlag> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the tri-axial information element <triaxialCableFlag>.
element.title: Tri-axial information description

element.name: descrTwist
element.definition: The element <descrTwist> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the twist element <twist>.
element.title: Twist information description

element.name: descrVoltage
element.definition: The element <descrVoltage> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the voltage information element <voltage>.
element.title: Voltage information description

element.name: descrWallThickness
element.definition: The element <descrWallThickness> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the wall thickness element <wallThickness>.
element.title: Wall thickness description

element.name: descrWire
element.definition: The element <descrWire> contains all relevant elements to describe the wire data elements of the project.
element.title: Wire data/field descriptions

element.name: descrWireColor
element.definition: The element <descrWireColor> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the wire color element <wireColor>.
element.title: Color information description

element.name: descrWireConnectionCode
element.definition: The element <descrWireConnectionCode> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the wire connection code element <wireConnectionCode>.
element.title: Wire connection code description

element.name: descrWireGauge
element.definition: The element <descrWireGauge> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the wire gauge element <wireGauge>.
element.title: Wire gauge description

element.name: descrWireInfoCircuitIdent
element.definition: The element <descrWireInfoCircuitIdent> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the circuit code child element <circuitIdent> of the element <wireInfo>.
element.title: Wire information circuit code description

element.name: descrWireInfoSectionIdent
element.definition: The element <descrWireInfoSectionIdent> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the wire section identification child element <sectionIdent> of the element <wireInfo>
element.title: Wire section identification description for the wire information branch

element.name: descrWireMaterial
element.definition: The element <descrWireMaterial> contains all relevant elements to describe the wire material data elements of the project.
element.title: Wire material data/field descriptions

element.name: descrWireNumber
element.definition: The element <descrWireNumber> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the wire number element <wireNumber>.
element.title: Wire number description

element.name: descrWireRoute
element.definition: The element <descrWireRoute> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the wire route lane code element <wireRoute>.
element.title: Wire route lane code description

element.name: descrWireSeqNumber
element.definition: The element <descrWireSeqNumber> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the wire sequential number element <wireSeqNumber>.
element.title: Wire sequential number description

element.name: descrWireType
element.definition: The element <descrWireType> contains child elements, which provide a project or an organization specific name, a brief description and a reference to other data modules or publications for the wire type element <wireType>.
element.title: Wire type description

element.name: detailedFaultDescr
element.definition: The element <detailedFaultDescr> contains the detailed description of the isolated fault.
element.title: Detailed description of the isolated fault

element.name: detectedFault
element.definition: The element <detectedFault> contains information on detected faults.
element.title: Detected faults

element.name: detectedFaultAlts
element.definition: The element <detectedFaultAlts> is an alternates group that provides the capability to group several alternate solutions of a detected faults. Refer to Chap 4.13.3.for information related to the use of alternates group in data module content.
element.title: Detected faults alternates group

element.name: detectedLruItem
element.definition: The element <detectedLruItem> contains the identification, name and abbreviation of the detected LRU.
element.title: Detected Line Replaceable Unit (LRU) item

element.name: detectedSruItem
element.definition: The element <detectedSruItem> contains the identification, name, abbreviation and any remark information of the detected SRU.
element.title: Detected Shop Replaceable Unit (SRU) item

element.name: detectionInfo
element.definition: The element <detectionInfo> contains the information for detecting the fault.
element.title: Fault detecting information

element.name: diagnosticProcess
element.definition: The element <diagnosticProcess> contains a textual description of the reason and a reference to a repair procedure or any locating and repairing tasks and information on the faulty LRU.
element.title: Diagnostics

element.name: diagnosticsReason
element.definition: the textual description of the of the cause of the failure
element.title: Diagnostics

element.name: dialog
element.definition: The element <dialog> allows the logic engine to interact with the user. It provides a means to obtain variable values from the user by asking a question. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.10.2 for expanded coverage of the element <dialog>.
element.title: Dialog

element.name: dialogAlts
element.definition: The element <dialogAlts> groups configuration or situation unique dialogs for display to the user. Only one or none will be displayed depending on the applicability assigned to each element <dialog> within the element <dialogAlts>. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.10.2 for expanded coverage of the element <dialogAlts>, and refer to Chap 4.13.3 and Para for more information about the alternates group concept.
element.title: Dialog alternates group

element.name: dialogGroup
element.definition:
The element <dialogGroup> allows the positioning of multiple dialog elements within one dialog either vertically or horizontally.
When multiple dialog types (menu, user entry, push button, and message) are used in a single dialog, the author can describe simple formatting instructions to the IETP to provide a logical flow to the dialog. The following elements and attributes can affect dialog layout:
element.title: Dialog layout

element.name: dispatchAddress
element.definition: The element <dispatchAddress> contains information about the originator of the comment such as the enterprise name, point of contact and the address from where the comment/response is sent.
element.title: Dispatch address

element.name: dispatchFileName
element.definition: to be found in DDN schema. The element <dispatchFileName> must contain the complete dispatch filename including TYPE and FORMAT, separated by hyphens and underscores as appropriate.
element.title: Data dispatch note

element.name: dispatchFrom
element.definition: to be found in DDN schema
element.title: Data dispatch note

element.name: dispatchPerson
element.definition: The element <dispatchPerson> contains information about the dispatch person who is the point of contact at the sending enterprise.
element.title: Dispatch person

element.name: dispatchTo
element.definition: to be found in DDN schema
element.title: Data dispatch note

element.name: displayName
element.definition: The element <displayName> defines text which can be used to help automated generation of the human-readable display text on an applicability annotation. Refer to Chap 7.8.
element.title: Display name

element.name: displayText
element.definition:
The element <displayText> provides a human-readable and printable branch of the applicability annotation.
For those projects or organizations that do not require computable applicability, the display text is the only part of the applicability annotation needed. For annotations, the repeatable simple paragraph element <simplePara> can be used. Refer to Chap 7.8 for the recommended format for the content of element <displayText>.
It is possible that auto-generated applicability statements are not completely consistent between processors. In projects or organizations deciding to use both the human-readable branch and one of the computable branches, the applicability annotation represented in both branches must match. In determining whether to populate the <displayText> a project or an organization must weigh this fact with the cost of maintaining authored <displayText>. Refer to Chap 7.8.
element.title: Human-readable applicability annotation branch

element.name: distributionPart
element.definition:
The element <distributionPart> is contained in the list of distribution parts element <distributionPartGroup>. The list of distribution parts is a child element of the list of the standard parts element <standardPartGroup>.
It contains information concerning the properties of distribution parts, like contacts and splices, used in the Product's wiring.
element.title: Element <distributionPart> and child elements

element.name: distributionPartGroup
element.definition: the list of distribution parts
element.title: Electrical standard parts

element.name: division
element.definition: the sending division of the enterprise, given as a simple text string
element.title: Enterprise

element.name: dmAddress
element.definition: The element <dmAddress> contains the unique identification and additional information supporting the data module.
element.title: Identification part

element.name: dmAddressItems
element.definition: The element <dmAddressItems> contains information that is supplementary to the data module identification but not part of the unique identifier.
element.title: Data module address items

element.name: dmCode
element.definition:
The element <dmCode> contains the data module code and forms part of the unique identifier of a data module. All data modules are allocated a data module code.
The data module code provides four top level pieces of information:
Establishing the data module code requires a data module coding strategy. Refer to Chap 4.3.
element.title: Data module code

element.name: dmElseSeq
element.definition:
The element <dmThenSeq> and the element <dmElseSeq> are identical and contain data module nodes, alternative sequences, branches, or loops to be traversed depending on the evaluation of the element <dmIf> expression.
Markup elements: <dmThenSeq> and <dmElseSeq>
element.title: Data module branch result

element.name: dmIdent
element.definition: The element <dmIdent> contains the unique identification of the data module.
element.title: Data module identification

element.name: dmIf
element.definition:
A branch sends the user down separate data paths based on state information such as configuration information, or how the user has answered questions. Branches can be nested.
The element <dmIf> uses the same logic as the "if-then-else" statement in a programming language. An element <dmIf> contains an expression that the logic engine evaluates to determine whether the element <dmThenSeq> or the element <dmElseSeq> is to be traversed.
The element <dmThenSeq> defines the data module node, alternative sequence, branch, or loops to be traversed if the element <dmIf> expression evaluates to TRUE. Element <dmThenSeq> is required.
The element <dmElseSeq> defines a data module node, alternative sequence, branch, or loops to be traversed if the element <dmIf> expression evaluates to FALSE. The element <dmElseSeq> is optional.
element.title: Data module branch

element.name: dml
element.definition:
For the planning, management and control of the content of the CSDB for individual projects the use of the following data management lists are recommended:
− Data Management Requirement List (DMRL), refer to Para 2
− CSDB Status List (CSL), refer to Para 3
Both are implemented in one and the same Schema.
element.title: Data management lists

element.name: dmlAddress
element.definition: The element <dmlAddress> consists of two blocks of information items used to uniquely address the data management list object.
element.title: Data management list address

element.name: dmlAddressItems
element.definition: to be found in DML schema. It uses the element <issueDate> to support identification of a data management list object
element.title: Data module list

element.name: dmlCode
element.definition:
The element <dmlCode> contains the data management list code. Together with the issue information, it gives an identification that is unique within the S1000D domain.
The data management list code is comprised of up to 29 alphanumeric characters and is built up as shown in . The minimum length is 17 characters.
Data management lists must be coded:
YY - YYYYY - A - XXXX - NNNNN (17 characters)
thru
YYYYYYYYYYYYYY - YYYYY - A - XXXX - NNNNN (29 characters)
Table 2 Data management list code
element.title: Data management list code

element.name: dmlContent
element.definition: The content of the data management list, contained in element <dmlContent>, consists of one or several <dmlEntry> subelements. Each of these subelements contains a CSDB object entry.
element.title: Data module list

element.name: dmlEntry
element.definition: The content of the data management list, contained in element <dmlContent>, consists of one or several <dmlEntry> subelements. Each of these subelements contains a CSDB object entry.
element.title: Data management list content

element.name: dmlIdent
element.definition: to be found in DML schema. It uses the element <dmlCode> and <issueInfo> to establish the unique identity of a data management list object
element.title: Data module list

element.name: dmLoop
element.definition:
A loop repeats a sequence of information as long as the loop condition (based on the state information) is true.
The element <dmLoop> provides a capability similar to programming language loops. It provides the syntax for creating WHILE (eg, WHILE a gap measurement < 1,5) or FOR NEXT (eg, FOR NEXT 5 times) loops, whichever applies to the situation. The element <dmLoop> contains an expression that is the WHILE condition for the loop and the element <dmSeq> (the content of the loop). The user will traverse the data module sequence as long as the expression evaluates to TRUE. The author is responsible for providing a way to update the variable or global property used in the WHILE condition so that the “truth” of the condition can change and the user can exit the loop. Optionally a loop can contain a loop entrance assertion (described by the element <variablePreSet>) and a loop end assertion (described by the element <assertion>) enabling counters to be implemented. The loop entrance assertion is made when (and only when) the user first enters the loop (eg, [Counter = 0]). The loop end assertion (eg, [Counter = Counter + 1]) is made each time the user reaches the end of the loop sequence described by the element <dmSeq>. The loop condition might be [Counter <= 5].
element.title: Data module loops

element.name: dmlRef
element.definition: The element <dmlRef> contains any references to other data management lists (eg, for partial data management requirement list).
element.title: Data management list reference

element.name: dmlRefGroup
element.definition: The element <dmlRefGroup> contains one or several references to data management lists to which the comment relates.
element.title: Data management list reference group

element.name: dmlRefIdent
element.definition: to be found in DML schema. References can be given by using the element <dmlRefIdent> within the element <dmlRef>. The element <dmlRefIdent> is similar to the element <dmlIdent> with the exception of an optional child element <issueInfo>.
element.title: Data module list

element.name: dmlStatus
element.definition: The element <dmlStatus> contains information about the status of the list.
element.title: Data management list status

element.name: dmNode
element.definition:
A data module node represents technical information that is to be displayed on one screen. This screen can be scrollable.
In a process data module, technical content is "wrapped" in the element <dmNode> structure allowing applicability (refer to Para ) and variable settings, using element <variablePreSet> (refer to Para ) and <variablePostSet> (refer to Para ), to be applied to the content. The element <title> provides a descriptive heading, which applies to the whole node.
It is important to remember that the element <dmNode> represents one screen of data, and if a dialog or message appears on the screen, it must appear after a procedural step or by itself. Reasons for this are (1) dialogs can alter the state information possibly changing the applicability of something after the dialog on the screen, and (2) dialogs have navigation choices which would allow the user to exit the screen before reading everything on it.
element.title: Data module node

element.name: dmNodeAlts
element.definition:
An alternative (or conditional) node is a group of objects, one or none of which will be applicable and thus displayed in a given situation.
The following discussion of the element <dmNodeAlts> applies to any other alternates group such as:
The alternates group concept is detailed in Chap 4.13.3.
The element <dmNodeAlts> (a group of node alternatives) provides the ability to group occurrences of the element <dmNode> together which apply in different contextual situations. Only one node or none of the group will be displayed. For example, three versions of a "disconnect connector" step can exist in the element <dmNodeAlts> due to equipment configuration differences. For any configuration, only one version of the step applies. The logic engine will evaluate the applicability expression for all occurrences of the element <dmNode> within the element <dmNodeAlts>. Only the first node whose applicability expression evaluates to TRUE will be displayed. There need not be an occurrence of the element <dmNode> within the element <dmNodeAlts> that is applicable to every possible situation. In such cases, the logic engine simply moves on to the next element <dmNode>. Every node in an alternative node must have applicability. Applicability on the alternative elements is optional in the content section of the Schema with the exception of the element <dmSeqAlts> within the element <dmIf>. For alternative processing, however, applicability is required on the alternative elements.
Applicability on the nodes of an alternates group must be mutually exclusive. Although the Schema does not require the alternative element <dmNode> to have mutually exclusive applicability (or the attribute applicRefId at all), it is important to understand the danger of overlapping applicability on alternative nodes. At most one will pass thru the logic engine to be displayed to the user, and if more than one applies, the user will miss something. A node with no applicability always applies. There can be reasons to author overlapping applicability within an alternates group, but it is not recommended.
element.title: Data module node alternates group

element.name: dmodule
element.definition: The common main container element for every data module.
element.title: Data Modules

element.name: dmRef
element.definition: The element <dmRef> contains the necessary information items to build a link to a destination data module. It contains the identification of the destination data module as well as information to control presentation and behavior of the reference/link to the data module or a fragment of the data module.
element.title: References to data modules

element.name: dmRefAddressItems
element.definition:
The element <dmRefAddressItems> contains additional identifying information about the destination data module.
The project or the organization must be aware of the implications of using the title and the issue date of the destination data module when referenced data modules are updated.
element.title: Complementary identifying information for a data module

element.name: dmRefGroup
element.definition: The element <dmRefGroup> contains one or several references to data modules to which the comment relates.
element.title: Data module reference group

element.name: dmRefIdent
element.definition:
The element <dmRefIdent> contains the items forming a unique identification of the destination data module.
The project or the organization must be aware of the implications of using issue and inwork numbers as well as language and country codes of the destination data module when referenced data modules are updated.
element.title: Identification of a referred data module

element.name: dmSegmentRef
element.definition: The element <dmSegmentRef> contains all necessary details to constitute a reference from some context, such as a data module or a publication module, to portions of a destination data module by the use of an XPath expression.
element.title: References to portions of a data module from a SCO content data module

element.name: dmSeq
element.definition: A data module sequence determines the order of steps, dialogs, external application calls, data module references, and/or conditional actions within the data module.
element.title: Data module sequences

element.name: dmSeqAlts
element.definition: An alternative (or conditional) sequence is a group of sequence orders where one or none will be applicable and thus executed in a given situation. The element <dmSeqAlts> contains one or more order of data module sequences. Each child element <dmSeq> has an attribute applicRefId referring to a pre-defined element <applic>. Refer to Para and Chap 4.13.3 for more information about the alternates group concept.
element.title: Data module alternative sequence

element.name: dmStatus
element.definition: The element <dmStatus> contains information about the status of the data module.
element.title: Status part

element.name: dmThenSeq
element.definition:
The element <dmThenSeq> and the element <dmElseSeq> are identical and contain data module nodes, alternative sequences, branches, or loops to be traversed depending on the evaluation of the element <dmIf> expression.
Markup elements: <dmThenSeq> and <dmElseSeq>
element.title: Data module branch result

element.name: dmTitle
element.definition: The element <dmTitle> contains the title of the data module. The data module title must give meaning to the Product identification and action elements of the data module code. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.1.5.
element.title: Data module title

element.name: documentIncorporation
element.definition: The element <documentIncorporation> lists the data modules and publications that this technical condition affects.
element.title: Document incorporation

element.name: effectivity
element.definition: The element <effectivity> identifies the range of products on which the item is fitted in this location.
element.title: Effectivity

element.name: electricalEquip
element.definition: The element <electricalEquip> is contained within the list of electrical equipment element <electricalEquipGroup>. It contains electrical equipment information of the Product's wiring.
element.title: Element <electricalEquip> and child elements

element.name: electricalEquipAlts
element.definition: The element <electricalEquipAlts>, within the list of electrical equipment element <electricalEquipGroup>, provides the capability to group several alternate solutions of electrical equipment data for different applicability annotations. Refer to Chap 4.13.3 for information related to the use of the alternates group mechanism in data module content.
element.title: Element <electricalEquipAlts>

element.name: electricalEquipConnection
element.definition: The element <electricalEquipConnection> gives information about the electrical connections that belong to a transverse link.
element.title: Electrical equipment connections

element.name: electricalEquipGroup
element.definition: the list of electrical equipment
element.title: wiring Data

element.name: electricalEquipState
element.definition: The electrical equipment state element <electricalEquipState> provides state information for each connection to some particular electrical equipment.
element.title: Electrical equipment state

element.name: electricalLogic
element.definition: The element <electricalLogic> provides an optional container for electrical states and internal connections between contacts for electrical or electronic equipment.
element.title: Electrical logic

element.name: electricalPotential
element.definition:
The element <electricalPotential> is used to order the wires based on project or organization specific rules, using the relation to termination module, block and shunt, or pole and position, and the contact identification.
There are two different cases for coding of the electrical potential information:
Equipment without internal logic. Equipment, such as connectors, earth bolts, etc, do not have an internal logic. The ascending values in element <contactOrderValue> represent the order of the equipment contacts to facilitate graphical presentation and connection analysis. Contacts of the equipment to which no physical wire is connected are included by wires with the wire state value "notactiv". For equipment without internal logic, the elements <terminationModuleGroupingValue>, <blockGroupingValue> and <shuntGroupingValue> must not be used.
Equipment with internal logic. Equipment, such as switches, relays and terminal junction modules have an internal logic. That means, the equipment has internal connections, which connect different contacts of the equipment electrically. For switches and relays, these electrical connections depend on the switch/relay position. The internal electrical connections are the basis for the coding of the elements <terminationModuleGroupingValue>, <blockGroupingValue> and <shuntGroupingValue>. Examples for the population of these elements are given in Para .
element.title: Electrical potential

element.name: electricalStateDescr
element.definition: The electrical state description element <electricalStateDescr> contains the narrative description of an electrical state, for example "ENERGIZED".
element.title: Electrical state description

element.name: elseIf
element.definition: The element <elseIf> contains an "if" statement to be used if another "if" condition is not met.
element.title: Else-if statement

element.name: email
element.definition: The element <email> contains contact email addresses for the business.
element.title: Email addresses

element.name: embeddedSpareDescr
element.definition:
The element <embeddedSpareDescr> contains the information of each spare item when included in a spare material set or the information of the original spare when included in a modified spare <spareDescr>.
The hierarchical relation between <spareDescr> and <embeddedSpareDescr> is:
element.title: Embedded spare description

element.name: embeddedSupplyDescr
element.definition:
The element <embeddedSupplyDescr> contains the information of each item included in a supply set.
The hierarchical relation between the element <supplyDescr> and the element <embeddedSupplyDescr> is the following:
element.title: Embedded supply description

element.name: embeddedSupportEquipDescr
element.definition:
The element <embeddedSupportEquipDescr> contains the information of each item included in a set of support equipment. In this case, the attribute materialUsage is set to "mu05" in the element <supportEquipDescr>.
The hierarchical relation between the element <supportEquipDescr> and the element <embeddedSupportEquipDescr> is the following:
element.title: Embedded support equipment description

element.name: emcCode
element.definition: The electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) code element <emcCode> identifies the EMC classification of a wire or a harness.
element.title: Electromagnetic compatibility code

element.name: emphasis
element.definition:
The element <emphasis> contains textual information (eg, a word, an expression or a sentence) to be highlighted.
To highlight a word, an expression or a sentence, bold text is the preferred method. Alternatively, the use of color is permitted. Capital (uppercase) letters, italics or underlining are not permitted to highlight text, except for legacy data. Refer to Chap 3.9.1.
element.title: Emphasis

element.name: enabledState
element.definition:
The element <enabledState> contains an expression that evaluates state variables to determine if data entry field input is permitted or inhibited (usually the data entry is visible, but dimmed or grayed out).
The element <enabledState> is evaluated when data input is entered within the dialog and before the dialog is submitted. Typically, this is used when a user selects a menu choice that requires additional data entry information, while other menu choices do not require the additional data entry.
An example is a dialog containing both a menu and a user entry dialog. The menu has two choices: integer (assertion is [datatype = "integer"]) and real (assertion is [datatype = "real"]). The user entry dialog requests a real precision value (enable expression is [datatype equal "real"]). If the integer choice is selected from the menu, the user entry is disabled. If the real choice is selected, the user entry is enabled.
element.title: User enabling/disabling entry

element.name: endItemCode
element.definition: The element <endItemCode> contains the end item code which typically consists of a three-character alphanumeric code. The end item code is another type of identifier, similar to NSN, CAGE Code, part number, etc.
element.title: End item code

element.name: endMatter
element.definition: The element <endMatter> contains end matter using the element <para>.
element.title: End matter statement

element.name: enterprise
element.definition: The element <enterprise> contains the name of the enterprise, and when applicable, the body within an enterprise, from where the comment/response is sent.
element.title: Enterprise

element.name: enterpriseGroup
element.definition: The element <enterpriseGroup> contains the supply product list of manufacturers, suppliers, etc.
element.title: Enterprise list

element.name: enterpriseIdent
element.definition: The element <enterpriseIdent> identifies an enterprise, thru its CAGE code.
element.title: Enterprise identification

element.name: enterpriseIdentAlt
element.definition: The element <enterpriseIdentAlt> provides an alternate method of identifying an enterprise where the enterprise does not have a CAGE code.
element.title: Alternate enterprise identification

element.name: enterpriseInfo
element.definition: The element <enterpriseInfo> contains information about a supplier of the supply.
element.title: Enterprise information

element.name: enterpriseLogo
element.definition:
The element <enterpriseLogo> contains logotypes of the manufacturer of the Product.
The first appearance of the element <symbol> will by default be the one presented on the title page. Refer to Chap 6.2.3.1.
The content is authored or derived from the source publication module.
element.title: Manufacturer's logotype

element.name: enterpriseName
element.definition: The element <enterpriseName> contains a text string identifying the enterprise.
element.title: Enterprise/manufacturer/supplier name

element.name: enterpriseRef
element.definition: The element <enterpriseRef> contains data about an enterprise and also allows referring to its position in the enterprise CIR. In the context of the part data, the element <enterpriseRef> can be used to indicate the authorized prime source supplier for the part or to specify additional suppliers.
element.title: Reference to an enterprise

element.name: enterpriseRepository
element.definition: The element <enterpriseRepository> contains a list of enterprise descriptions.
element.title: Enterprise list repository

element.name: enterpriseSpec
element.definition: The element <enterpriseSpec> contains information about the manufacturer of the product such as the enterprise name, the business unit and address.
element.title: Manufacturer

element.name: enterpriseUnit
element.definition: the business unit of the division, given as a simple text string
element.title: Enterprise

element.name: entityControlNumber
element.definition: The element <entityControlNumber> must contain only the CONTROLNUMBER without TYPE and FORMAT (ie, without any prefix or postfix) separated by hyphens as appropriate.
element.title: Data dispatch note

element.name: entry
element.definition: The element <entry> (table entry) represents a table cell.
element.title: Table entry

element.name: enumeration
element.definition:
The element <enumeration> provides the allowable values for the product attribute. The allowable values can be used to assist the technical author in creating applicability annotations.
The element <enumeration> is optional and repeatable. If there are multiple values and ranges to define, several methods can be employed, including the following:
- single element <enumeration> with attribute applicPropertyValues representing the entire set of values and ranges
- multiple element <enumeration> with attribute applicPropertyValues representing a single value or range
element.title: Enumeration

element.name: equip
element.definition: The element <equip> contains the identification of an individual piece of equipment associated with the task.
element.title: Equipment

element.name: equipDescrRef
element.definition: The element <equipDescrRef> contains references to other parts of the electrical system publications, which give further information about this electrical equipment, such as the description of how it is made.
element.title: Electrical equipment description reference

element.name: equipGroup
element.definition: The element <equipGroup> contains the grouping of the equipment that used in or associated with the task.
element.title: Equipment group

element.name: equipmentNotAvailable
element.definition: The element <equipmentNotAvailable> contains the condition of the equipment (shortages, malfunctions, etc) that makes equipment not ready or not available for use. This element is aligned with the checklist item or paragraph for which the equipment not ready/available is applicable. This element includes a required element <para>.
element.title: Equipment not available

element.name: equipName
element.definition: The element <equipName> describes the electrical equipment. A standard name, like SWITCH, can be used or a name that defines the electrical equipment in conjunction with its use on the system, for example BATTERY MASTER SWITCH.
element.title: Electrical equipment name

element.name: estimatedTime
element.definition: The element <estimatedTime> contains the estimate of aggregation of all the time that the person will be required to complete the task. The estimated time must be given in man-hours with maximum one decimal (eg, 2,4 h). The time and unit of measure must be entered.
element.title: Estimated time

element.name: evaluate
element.definition:
The element <evaluate> is used to group tests together and provide the logical operation to apply to the test results. The element <evaluate> must be used whenever more than one test, element <assert>, is required. The element <evaluate> can contain other instances of the element <evaluate> and is therefore recursive. This allows for very complicated applicability annotations to be represented with a simple structure consisting of only two element types.
To process the element <evaluate> all child elements are evaluated, each providing a Boolean value of "true" or "false". Then the logical "and" or "or" operation is performed on the results, providing a single Boolean result. A result of the value "true" means the associated content is valid and a result of the value "false" means the associated content is not valid. The method of presenting validity is a project or an organization decision. Some projects or organizations will remove invalid content while others will want to de-emphasize the invalid content in some way.
element.title: Grouping applicability tests - evaluate

element.name: example
element.definition: The element <example> provides markup and/or narrative examples illustrating the effect of the business rule decisions with a corresponding definition.
element.title: Example

element.name: excludedModification
element.definition: The element <excludedModification> contains an indication that the modification is not covered even though it was actually required.
element.title: Excluded modification

element.name: exportControl
element.definition: The element <exportControl> is used to define any government regulations concerning access to the data module.
element.title: Export control

element.name: exportRegistrationCode
element.definition: The element <exportRegistrationCode> is used to indicate a coded value representing an export control property that applies to the content of the data module.
element.title: Export registration code

element.name: exportRegistrationStmt
element.definition: The element <exportRegistrationStmt> is used to specify either a full or a partial export registration statement describing the export status of the data module content.
element.title: Export registration statement

element.name: expression
element.definition:
Expressions are combinations of variables or global properties and operators. The logic engine evaluates expressions primarily in order to determine what is displayed to the user as in applicability ([Model = "T"]) and branching ([If Error Condition = "4"]) and loop (WHILE [Counter < "5"]) conditions. Expressions are also used to assign a value to a variable or global property ([Ave = Sum / 3]). Refer to Chap 7.6.1.4 for detailed information.
The element <expression> defines something to be evaluated by the logic engine. Expressions are used to assign values to variables in assertions, eg, the value of the expression [Counter plus 1] might be assigned to the variable Counter. They are also used in applicability, branches, and other constructs to determine what is displayed to the user. In element <applic>, for example, if the expression [Item deployed equal TRUE] evaluates to TRUE, the data item containing the applicability is displayed and vice versa. Expressions are authored by combining variables (or other expressions) and values with binary or unary operators. In their simplest form, an expression can be just a variable/global property or a value. The element <assert> can also be used to simplify the authoring of complicated expressions.
element.title: Expressions

element.name: externalApplication
element.definition: The element <externalApplication> is a mechanism for launching an external application in the midst of a process data module. This application might be a wiring data viewer, a calculator, a diagnostic test, etc. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.10.3 for expanded coverage of the element <externalApplication>.
element.title: External application

element.name: externalAssert
element.definition: The element <externalAssert> is based on the element <assert>. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.3. An external assertion identifyies a product attribute, a product attribute value or a condition defined in an ACT data module using the element <applicCrossRefTableRef>.
element.title: External assertions

element.name: externalCond
element.definition: The element <externalCond> specifies a condition defined in another CCT. This element must be used only by projects or organizations implementing the ACT catalog. Refer to Chap 4.14.4. In this case, the ACT catalog must provide the reference to the condition definition.
element.title: External condition

element.name: externalProductAttribute
element.definition: The element <externalProductAttribute> specifies a product attribute defined in another ACT. This element must be used only by projects or organizations implementing the ACT catalog. Refer to Chap 4.14.4. In this case, the ACT catalog must provide the reference to the product attribute definition.
element.title: External product attribute

element.name: externalPubCode
element.definition: The element <externalPubCode> contains, as textual content, a code that identifies a publication or document by a non-S1000D identity, such as an ISBN code or similar.
element.title: Identifying code of an external document

element.name: externalPubIssue
element.definition: The element <externalPubIssue> contains, as text content, issue identification of a non-S1000D publication or document.
element.title: Issue information of an external document

element.name: externalPubIssueDate
element.definition:
The element <externalPubIssueDate> contains date information about the referenced publication.
If the <externalPubIssueDate> contains a date in textual form, then that text will be displayed to the user. If only the attributes are provided, then it must be displayed in the ISO 8601 calendar date format YYYY-MM or YYYY-MM-DD. If the attribute month of <externalPubIssueDate> is populated then the attribute year must also be populated. If the attribute day is populated then the attribute month must also be populated. Refer to default BREX rule BREX-S1-00061.
element.title: Issue date of an external publication

element.name: externalPubIssueInfo
element.definition: The element <externalPubIssueInfo> contains issue information concerning the referred non-S1000D publication or document.
element.title: Issue information of an external document

element.name: externalPubRef
element.definition: The element <externalPubRef> contains the identification of a referenced non-S10000D publication or document.
element.title: References to external documents

element.name: externalPubRefAddressItems
element.definition: The element <externalPubRefAddressItems> contains, as textual content, additional information potentially useful to identify the non-S1000D destination publication or document.
element.title: Complementary identifying information for an external document

element.name: externalPubRefIdent
element.definition: The element <externalPubRefIdent> contains the identification of a non-S1000D publication or document.
element.title: Identification of an external document

element.name: externalPubTitle
element.definition: The element <externalPubTitle> contains, as textual content, the title of a non-S1000D publication or document.
element.title: Title of an external document

element.name: externalSco
element.definition: The element <externalSco> is used within a SCO content data module to establish data links to existing SCO that are not managed in accordance with S1000D. The linking mechanism is the same as that used to refer from a data module to any non-S10000D publication or document.
element.title: External SCO

element.name: externalScoIssue
element.definition: to be found in complex Elements schema
element.title: SCORM

element.name: externalScoIssueInfo
element.definition: to be found in complex Elements schema
element.title: SCORM

element.name: extraResources
element.definition: The element <extraResources> is used to refer to figures, tables and multimedia objects required by the training step.
element.title: Extra resources

element.name: falseValue
element.definition: The element <falseValue> is an expression operand that is set to "false" with the data type Boolean.
element.title: Process data module - Logic engine

element.name: fastener
element.definition: The element <fastener> is used to store access point fastener information.
element.title: Fastener

element.name: fastenerQuantity
element.definition: The element <fastenerQuantity> contains the quantity of fasteners.
element.title: Fastener quantity

element.name: fastenerType
element.definition: The element <fastenerType> contains the type of the fastener.
element.title: Fastener type

element.name: fault
element.definition: the basic fault presented through the attribute faultCode
element.title: Warning or malfunction

element.name: faultCond
element.definition: the potential fault condition
element.title: Detailed description of the isolated fault

element.name: faultContext
element.definition: the textual description of the context in which the fault is observed
element.title: Context and isolation information

element.name: faultDescr
element.definition: The element <faultDescr> contains the description of the isolated fault.
element.title: Description of the isolated fault

element.name: faultEquip
element.definition: the equipment involved in the malfunction
element.title: Detailed description of the isolated fault

element.name: faultIsolation
element.definition: The fault isolation branch of the Schema identifies and describes the fault and gives the diagnostic path to isolate a faulty item.
element.title: Fault isolation

element.name: faultIsolationProcedure
element.definition: The element <faultIsolationProcedure> contains the detailed fault isolation procedure including fault code, fault description, and the possible causes of the malfunction that can be isolated through the fault isolation procedure.
element.title: Fault isolation procedure

element.name: faultIsolationRef
element.definition: the reference to information needed to isolate the detected/observed fault
element.title: Isolate detected faults

element.name: faultIsolationTest
element.definition: The element <faultIsolationTest> contains the test description, test parameters and the test procedure.
element.title: Fault isolation test performance

element.name: faultMessageBody
element.definition: the main part of the fault message
element.title: Detailed description of the isolated fault

element.name: faultMessageIndication
element.definition: the indication part of the fault message (eg, "temperature")
element.title: Detailed description of the isolated fault

element.name: faultReporting
element.definition: The element <faultReporting> contains information to enable technician or computerized maintenance systems to indicate faults through data modules.
element.title: Fault reporting

element.name: faultySubSystem
element.definition: the faulty subsystem
element.title: Detailed description of the isolated fault

element.name: faxNumber
element.definition: The element <faxNumber> contains contact fax numbers for the business.
element.title: Fax numbers

element.name: feedThru
element.definition: If a wire passes thru equipment (eg, a current transformer), this is identified by using the feed-thru element <feedThru>.
element.title: Feed-thru information

element.name: fieldName
element.definition: The element <fieldName> contains the name of the data field (ie, the project or the organization specific naming of electrical data).
element.title: Data/field name

element.name: figure
element.definition:
This element contains the figure. The figure consists of a title and one or more illustrations sheets, using the element <graphic>, and a figure title. It can also contain a legend.
For specific rules and guidance for the preparation of illustrations, refer to Chap 3.9.2.
In all cases, figures must be placed as near as possible to, and preferably immediately following, the related text.
element.title: Figure

element.name: figureAlts
element.definition: The element <figureAlts> is an alternate group that provides the capability to group several alternatives to a figure.
element.title: Figure alternates

element.name: figureAltsIdent
element.definition: The element <figureAltsIdent> identifies a figure alternates group.
element.title: Figure alternates group identifier

element.name: figureIdent
element.definition: The element <figureIdent> identifies a figure.
element.title: Figure identifier

element.name: finishingInfo
element.definition: The element <finishingInfo> contains the finishing information for the connection of wires to a contact.
element.title: Finishing information

element.name: firstName
element.definition: The element <firstName> contains the first or given name of the individual contact.
element.title: First name

element.name: firstVerification
element.definition: The element <firstVerification> identifies a data module which has been first verified.
element.title: First verification

element.name: fitmentCode
element.definition: The element <fitmentCode> indicates whether an item can be fitted as supplied.
element.title: Fitment code

element.name: foldout
element.definition: The element <foldout> is used to contain a figure, or table which is larger than the default size.
element.title: Foldouts

element.name: footnote
element.definition:
The element <footnote> contains bibliographic references or explanations which would take too much space or in any other way would be annoying for the reader in the running text.
The element <footnote> is a wrapper around the contents of a footnote. The element <footnote> typically generates a marker (eg, a superscripted number) at the place in the flow of text where it occurs. The footnote itself is then generally presented at the bottom of the page or at the end of the table.
Footnotes are used in tables (table footnotes) or in regular text and titles (inline footnotes).
Footnotes must not
− be used for references and cross-references
− include footnotes
element.title: Footnote

element.name: footnoteRef
element.definition: The element <footnoteRef> contains the link information to a previously defined footnote. This link information is used each time when referring to the same footnote.
element.title: Footnote reference

element.name: footnoteRemarks
element.definition: The element <footnoteRemarks> contains either textual content of remarks using the element <simplePara> or internal reference to footnotes using the element<footnoteRef>. It allows internal references to a target element<footnote>.
element.title: Footnote remarks

element.name: format
element.definition:
The physical or digital manifestation of the resource. Typically, element <dc:format> includes the media type or dimensions of the resource. Element <dc:format> can be used to determine the software, hardware or other equipment needed to display or operate the resource. Examples of dimensions include size and duration. Recommended best practice is to select a value from a controlled vocabulary (eg, the list of Internet media types defining computer media formats).
S1000D assigns the fixed value "text/xml" to this Dublin core element.
element.title: Dublin core elements

element.name: frequency
element.definition: The element <frequency> contains the frequency of wire material in relation to the attenuation.
element.title: Frequency

element.name: frequencyAttenuation
element.definition: The element <frequencyAttenuation> contains the frequency and attenuation information of wire material.
element.title: Frequency and attenuation

element.name: frequencyCharacteristics
element.definition: The element <frequencyCharacteristics> contains alternating current frequency characteristics of wire material.
element.title: Frequency characteristics

element.name: fromEquip
element.definition: The content model electrical equipment defines the child elements of the elements <fromEquip> and <toEquip>, which contain the wire connection information for the two wire ends.
element.title: Content model electrical equipment

element.name: frontMatter
element.definition:
The Front matter Schema contains three branches supporting the following Front matter types:
− Title page.
− Table of contents.
− Highlights and various Front matter lists.
element.title: Front matter

element.name: frontMatterDmEntry
element.definition: The element <frontMatterDmEntry> contains the data module entry. The order of the front matter data module entries must follow structure of the publication (= TOC).
element.title: Front matter data module entry

element.name: frontMatterExternalPubEntry
element.definition: The element <frontMatterExternalPubEntry> contains the non-S1000D publication (external publication) entry.
element.title: Front matter non-S1000D publication entry

element.name: frontMatterInfo
element.definition:
The element <frontMatterInfo> contains miscellaneous Front matter information (eg, manufacturer's information, error reporting, availability statements or general purpose notices including an introductory title). Several sets of Front matter information and types can be given.
The titles must be coded with attribute emphasisType value em01.
element.title: Miscellaneous information - Front matter information

element.name: frontMatterList
element.definition:
The element <frontMatterList> contains the information for the:
− List of effective pages - LOEP
− List of effective data modules - LOEDM
− Highlights - HIGH
The Schema also supports the capturing and representation of publication lists for the List of applicable publications.
element.title: Front matter lists

element.name: frontMatterPmEntry
element.definition: The element <frontMatterPmEntry> contains the publication module entry.
element.title: Front matter publication module entry

element.name: frontMatterSubList
element.definition:
The element <frontMatterSubList> contains the information for the:
− List of effective pages - LOEP.
− List of effective data modules - LOEDM.
− Highlights - HIGH (without and with updating instructions)
The entries can be grouped in one or more front matter lists (<frontMatterSubList>) each having a title (<title>) and one or more introductory paragraphs (<reducedPara>).
element.title: Front matter sublist

element.name: frontMatterTableOfContent
element.definition:
The element <frontMatterTableOfContent> contains the Table of contents information. Two types of Table of contents are possible:
− A non-hierarchical (flat) structure.
− A hierarchical structure.
Both types list the data modules and any publications or external publications in the order given by the source publication module.
element.title: Table of contents

element.name: frontMatterTitlePage
element.definition:
The element <frontMatterTitlePage> contains the title page information.
Rules for presentation of title pages in accordance with the S1000D standard page-oriented presentation is given in Chap 6.2.3.1.
element.title: Title page

element.name: fullNatoStockNumber
element.definition: The element <fullNatoStockNumber> contains the NATO stock number (NSN) ready for presentation.
element.title: NATO stock number

element.name: functionalDescrRef
element.definition: The element <functionalDescrRef> contains references to other parts of the electrical system publications for further information about a wire, harness or electrical equipment (eg, a description of how it is made and its function).
element.title: Functional description reference

element.name: functionalItem
element.definition:
The element <functionalItem> contains information that describes the functional item and provides further information related to the functional item based on the Product configuration.
Configuration consistency between the various alternate functional items must be ensured. This means that, for a given Product configuration and under given operational conditions, only one alternate functional item is applicable at one time for a given functional item.
element.title: Alternate functional item

element.name: functionalItemAlts
element.definition: The element <functionalItemAlts> is a group of functional item alternates.
element.title: Functional item alternate group

element.name: functionalItemCode
element.definition: The element <functionalItemCode> contains a code that represents the functional breakdown.
element.title: Functional item code

element.name: functionalItemFamily
element.definition: The element <functionalItemFamily> contains the family equipment the functional item belongs to, for example a technological category that characterizes it.
element.title: Functional item family

element.name: functionalItemIdent
element.definition:
The element <functionalItemIdent>, contrary to the attribute id, semantically identifies the functional item as a result of its attributes.
A functional item can be used to uniquely identify an item performing a function in a given system at a given position for a given Product.
For example a functional item can be the third pump in a hydraulic system for a given Product. Depending on the Product instance, this functional item can be mechanical or electrical and, so, can perform the function of a hydraulic pump.
The functional item is also known as the reference designator.
The codification of functional item identifier is up to the project or the organization.
element.title: Functional item identifier

element.name: functionalItemRef
element.definition:
The element <functionalItemRef> contains a reference to the functional item’s CIR data module.
A functional item can be used to uniquely identify an item that performs a function, in a system that is part of a Product.
For example a functional item can be the third pump in a hydraulic system of a Product. Depending on the Product instance, this functional item can be mechanical or electrical, and so, can perform the function of a hydraulic pump.
The functional item is also known as the reference designator.
element.title: Functional item reference

element.name: functionalItemRefGroup
element.definition:
The element <functionalItemRefGroup> provides relationships and the types of relationships with other functional items (eg, sub-functional items or software functional items related to a hardware functional item).
Depending on the relationships, it can depend on the product instances or not. As a result, this element can be found at both container and alternate levels.
element.title: References to other functional items

element.name: functionalItemRepository
element.definition: The element <functionalItemRepository> provides a list of functional items and their associated properties.
element.title: Functional item repository

element.name: functionalItemSpec
element.definition:
The element <functionalItemSpec> contains the associated properties of one functional item.
Some properties of the functional item can vary from one product instance to another. Such properties are managed under the functional item alternates (element <functionalItemAlts>), which can contain a reference to an applicability annotation.
For some properties appearing at both levels (eg, <name> or <shortName>), the following rule must be applied: if the property is fulfilled in the alternate element, it supersedes the generic value stored in the parent element.
element.title: Functional item specification

element.name: functionalPhysicalAreaIdent
element.definition:
The element <functionalPhysicalAreaIdent> semantically identifies the functional and/or physical area as a result of its attributes.
If values "0", "00" or "0000" are filled in the optional attributes subSystemCode, subSubSystemCode and assyCode, they are considered as significant values. In addition to the functional and/or physical area identifier, the system difference code can be specified for each functional and/or physical area.
element.title: Functional and/or physical area identifier

element.name: functionalPhysicalAreaRef
element.definition: The element <functionalPhysicalAreaRef> is used to mark up a functional and/or physical area (outside of its specification in the functional and/or physical areas CIR) and also allows referring to its specification in the functional and/or physical areas CIR.
element.title: Functional and/or physical area

element.name: functionalPhysicalAreaRefGroup
element.definition:
The element <functionalPhysicalAreaRefGroup> reflects the functional and/or physical breakdown. If used, it must only contain the links from a functional and/or physical area to its direct child elements.
This means that a system can only refer to subsystems with the same system code values, a subsystem can only refer to sub-subsystems with the same system and subsystem code values, etc.
element.title: References to other functional and/or physical areas

element.name: functionalPhysicalAreaRepository
