element.definition: The element <sbSupportEquipSet> contains the information about support equipment grouped by set and involved in the Service bulletin.
element.title: Support equipment set

element.name: sbSupportEquipsList
element.definition: The element <sbSupportEquipsList> contains the list of support equipment (set, individual, external) involved in the Service bulletin.
element.title: List of support equipment

element.name: sbTaskType
element.definition: The element <sbTaskType> gives the main type of activity the Service bulletin describes.
element.title: Service bulletin task type

element.name: sbTimeAssessment
element.definition: The element <sbTimeAssessment> contains the time information (duration, elapsed time) necessary to perform the Service bulletin. Time information is related to the different product configurations described in within the Service bulletin.
element.title: Time assessment

element.name: sbTimeCompliance
element.definition: The element <sbTimeCompliance> contains the limit to accomplish the Service bulletin (basic limit, grace period, repetitive interval).
element.title: Time compliance

element.name: sbTimeInfo
element.definition: The element <sbTimeInfo> contains the time information like duration and elapsed time, based on applicability and task to perform.
element.title: Time information

element.name: sbTopic
element.definition: The element <sbTopic> contains the necessary information for descriptive Service bulletin main topics (revision information, summary, planning information and additional information).
element.title: Service bulletin topic

element.name: sbTopicContent
element.definition: The element <sbTopicContent> contains the descriptive content of a Service bulletin topic.
element.title: Service bulletin topic content

element.name: scoContent
element.definition: The element <scoContent> is the container for SCORM content data of the SCO content data module. This element can be populated with references to external SCO (not defined using S1000D data), or by a sequence of training steps.
element.title: SCO content

element.name: scoEntry
element.definition: The element <scoEntry> contains the title and references to the data modules that comprises the content of a learning event. It also contains LOM information to describe the referenced content. It is the central element for a SCORM content module package and can be defined recursively. This allows SCORM content package module structures in variable depth.
element.title: SCO entry

element.name: scoEntryItem
element.definition:
The element <scoEntryItem> contains a reference to a data module that comprises the content of a learning event. It also contains LOM information to describe the referenced content. As shown in the example below, the element <scoEntry> can be nested within the element <scoEntryItem> in order to define the hierarchical structure of the course or learning event.
A SCO entry can be composed by references to the data modules containing the content that must be included or to sub SCO entry definitions or both. References can be made to any type of content data module within the element <scoEntryItem>.
element.title: SCO entry item

element.name: scoEntryTitle
element.definition: The element <scoEntryTitle> contains the title of the SCO entry within the SCORM content package module.
element.title: SCO entry title

element.name: scormContentPackage
element.definition: The element <scormContentPackage> contains two major element wrappers: the element <identAndStatusSection> describes training life cycle and configuration information; the element <content> contains pointers to SCO content information which aggregates data modules used in a product deliverable.
element.title: Content of the SCORM content package module

element.name: scormContentPackageAddress
element.definition: The element <scormContentPackageAddress> contains the identification, title and issue details of the SCORM content package module.
element.title: SCORM content package module address

element.name: scormContentPackageAddressItems
element.definition: The element <scormContentPackageAddressItems> contains information that is supplementary to the SCORM content package module identification but not part of the unique identifier.
element.title: SCORM content package module address items

element.name: scormContentPackageCode
element.definition: The element <scormContentPackageCode>contains the SCORM content package module code that forms part of the unique identifier of the SCORM content package module.
element.title: SCORM content package module code

element.name: scormContentPackageIdent
element.definition: The element <scormContentPackageIdent> contains the unique identification of the SCORM content package module..
element.title: SCORM content package module identification

element.name: scormContentPackageMedia
element.definition: The element <scormContentPackageMedia> contains the description of the media on which the SCORM content package module is delivered.
element.title: SCORM content package module media

element.name: scormContentPackageStatus
element.definition: The element <scormContentPackageStatus>contains the status information of the SCORM content package module.
element.title: SCORM content package module status

element.name: scormContentPackageTitle
element.definition: The element <scormContentPackageTitle> contains the title of the SCORM content package module.
element.title: SCORM content package module title

element.name: screen
element.definition: The element <screen> and its attributes contain information about the type of the wire in a shielding situation and the identification of the screen to which the wire end is connected.
element.title: Screen/shield information

element.name: screenCount
element.definition: The element <screenCount> contains the number of screens of the wire material.
element.title: Screen count

element.name: screenGroup
element.definition: The element <screenGroup> is used as a container for the screen/shield information.
element.title: List of screens

element.name: screenOrder
element.definition: The element <screenOrder> contains an ascending numerical value, which represents the order of the wires connected to an equipment for graphical presentation of the shielding situation.
element.title: Shielding order

element.name: secondVerification
element.definition: The element <secondVerification> identifies a data module which has been second verified. Data modules cannot be only second verified. They must also be first verified. Refer to Chap 3.7.
element.title: Second verification

element.name: sectionIdent
element.definition:
Wires which are connected by non-functional breaks such as connectors, terminal junction modules, splices, etc, often get the same wire identification number and different section identifications. In this case, the wire section identification element must be populated with the identifiers for these different sections.
For projects that do not use the wire section identification in the identification of the wire itself, the wire section identification element <sectionIdent> is additionally available in the wire information element <wireInfo>.
element.title: Wire section identification

element.name: security
element.definition: The element <security> contains the security classification and restrictive marking of the complete data module, data mangement list or publication module, and its contained or associated illustrations.
element.title: Security classification

element.name: selectOrManufacture
element.definition: The element <selectOrManufacture> identifies the range of items to be used for the selection, manufacture, rework or repair of an item. Items must be identified using the CSN, figure number and item number or just the item number as appropriate.
element.title: Select or manufacture

element.name: selectOrManufactureFromIdent
element.definition: The element <selectOrManufactureFromIdent> indicates that an item must be selected to suit specific operating conditions, or that the item can be locally manufactured.
element.title: Select or manufacture from identifier

element.name: send
element.definition: The element <send> defines parameters to send to the called application. The element <send> contains an optional element <sendName> and the element <variableRef>, the element <globalPropertyRef> or the element <stringValue>. Send parameters can be either local variable or global property values or strings. The element <sendName> defines the parameter name used by the external application. When used in conjunction with the element <variableRef>, the element <globalPropertyRef> or the element <stringValue>, it clearly defines each sending parameter.
element.title: Send

element.name: sendName
element.definition: The element <sendName> contains a character string giving the name of a parameter sent to and used by a called external application.
element.title: Send name

element.name: sequentialList
element.definition:
The element <sequentialList> contains sequential lists, which are also known as ordered lists. It can contain applicability information, a title and the list items themselves.
In a sequential list, a list item can consist of one or more paragraphs. A sequential list, when formatted, is numbered with Arabic numerals. Refer to Chap 6.2.2.
Sequential lists must not be used to provide procedural step information. Refer to default BREX rule BREX-S1-00065.
Only one sequential (ordered) list must be placed under a numbered title or paragraph (subheading). Refer to default BREX rule BREX-S1-00066.
Sequential lists are limited to a maximum of two levels. Refer to default BREX rule BREX-S1-00064.
element.title: Sequential lists

element.name: serialNumber
element.definition: The element <serialNumber> contains the listing of a single serial number or a range.
element.title: Serial number

element.name: service
element.definition: The element <service> identifies the user service to which specific data is applicable. The text content of the element has three characters. The first two characters must be populated with the user nation code. The third character is project or organization specific.
element.title: Service

element.name: setFunction
element.definition:
The element <setFunction> is a set function that evaluates the element <expression> based on the attribute "setAction" with the following definitions–
• "empty" – Returns "true" if the operand set is empty otherwise returns "false".
• "sizeOf" – Returns an integer value that is the number of members in the operand set.
element.title: Expression evaluation

element.name: setOperator
element.definition:
he element <setOperator> is an operator between two set expressions in the Process DM. The operator evaluates the two element <expression>s based on the attribute "setOperation" with following definitions–
• "add" – Creates a set containing all members of the old set plus the operand value.
• "disjoint" – Returns "true" if the intersection of the two sets is empty otherwise returns "false".
• "equal" – Returns "true" if the values in the set are the identical otherwise returns "false".
• "intersection" – Creates a set that contains only values that are members of both operand set datatypes.
• "member" – Returns "true" if operand value is contained in the set otherwise returns "false".
• "notEqual" – Returns "true" if the values in the set are different otherwise returns "false".
• "remove" – Creates a set containing all members of the old set minus the operand value.
• "setDifference" – Creates a set which is the difference of the two sets.
• "subset" – Returns "true" if all elements of the first set are contained in the second otherwise returns "false".
• "union" – Creates a set containing members of both sets in the expression. Members contained in both sets will be contained in the new set only once.
element.title: Expression evaluation

element.name: setValue
element.definition: The element <setValue> is an expression operand that is set to the data type set. Initializes a set to one or more of one data type (real, integer, or string) or the empty set.
element.title: Expression evaluation

element.name: shelfLife
element.definition: The element <shelfLife> contains information about the shelf life of the supply.
element.title: Shelf life

element.name: shippingInfo
element.definition: The element <shippingInfo> contains the shipping information for the supply, if any. They are divided into four types of information.
element.title: Shipping information

element.name: shortDescr
element.definition: The element <shortDescr> contains a short description of the learning assessment portion of the course or lesson.
element.title: Short description

element.name: shortExternalPubTitle
element.definition: the non-S1000D publication title can be expressed by the elements <externalPubTitle> (M) and <shortExternalPubTitle> (O)
element.title: Non-S1000D publication entry (external publication entry)

element.name: shortName
element.definition:
The element <shortName> contains an abbreviated alternate name of the corresponding element <name>. This short form of the name is meant to be presented in the narrative of the data module to make the reading easier.
The project or organization must make sure that any used short name is unique to avoid mistakes which could result in death or injuries to personnel, or damage to the Product.
element.title: Shortname

element.name: shortPmTitle
element.definition: The element <shortPmTitle> contains the short title of the publication or an extension to the publication title like "- Volume No. 2" defined by the project.
element.title: Short publication module title

element.name: shrinkSleeve
element.definition:
The element <shrinkSleeve> is contained in the list of shrink sleeves element <shrinkSleeveGroup>. The list of shrink sleeves is a child element of the list of the standard parts element <standardPartGroup>.
It contains information concerning the properties of shrink sleeves used in the Product's wiring.
element.title: Element <shrinkSleeve> and child elements

element.name: shrinkSleeveGroup
element.definition: the list of shrink sleeves
element.title: Electrical standard parts

element.name: shuntGroupingValue
element.definition:
The element <shuntGroupingValue> is only used for equipment with internal logic. It provides grouping information for contacts that belong to the same shunt/bus of a termination module or the same position of a switch or a relay.
It must contain an identical alphanumerical value for all these contacts.
element.title: Shunt grouping information

element.name: siblingPlugIdent
element.definition: The element <siblingPlugIdent> contains the functional item of the mate connector in the subordinate element <functionalItemRef>. That means, the plug gets as twin connector identification the functional item of the receptacle, and the receptacle gets as twin connector identification the functional item of the plug. Functional items are also known as reference designators.
element.title: Twin connector identification

element.name: signalInfo
element.definition: The element <signalInfo> contains information about the signals on a wire. This can be the signal name, the effective voltage, the effective current or the waveform of the signal.
element.title: Signal information

element.name: simplePara
element.definition: The element <simplePara> contains a minimum set of text elements. It is used where the full set of elements that are available in a full paragraph (in the element <para>) is not required. Simple paragraphs are used for example in the elements <displayText> and <remarks>.
element.title: Simple paragraph

element.name: simpleRefPara
element.definition: The element <simpleRefPara> contains a minimum set of text elements. It is used where the full set of elements that are available in a full paragraph (in the element <para>) is not required, but a reference to a data module, a publication module or a non-S1000D publication required. Simple paragraphs with reference are used for example in the elements <reasonForUpdate>.
element.title: Simple paragraph with reference

element.name: simpleRemark
element.definition: The element <simpleRemark> contains general remarks about the shipping information related to the supply.
element.title: General remark

element.name: SITA
element.definition: The element <SITA> contains the address supplied in the aviation industry by the Société Internationale de Télécommunications Aéronautiques (SITA).
element.title: SITA address

element.name: size
element.definition: The element <size> contains the size information of a wire material, shrink sleeve or of a conduit.
element.title: Size

element.name: skillLevel
element.definition: The element <skillLevel> contains the skill level to which the data module has been written.
element.title: Skill level

element.name: sleeve
element.definition: The element <sleeve> contains information about a harness sleeve.
element.title: Harness sleeve information

element.name: sleeveDiameter
element.definition: The element <sleeveDiameter> contains the minimum and maximum diameter of a solder sleeve. Solder sleeves are normally defined by using several diameters (eg, diameters of the sleeve, or entry diameters). If this element is used, it must be defined which diameter is given.
element.title: Sleeve diameter

element.name: sleeveGroup
element.definition: The element <sleeveGroup> contains a list of sleeves that are used on the harness.
element.title: List of harness sleeves

element.name: snsAssy
element.definition: The element <snsAssy> contains the specification of each assembly defined for the SNS sub-subsystem concerned.
element.title: SNS assembly code

element.name: snsCode
element.definition: The element <snsCode> contains a code used to define a component in an SNS structure. The code concerned is represented as text content. Depending on the context it can be either the code of a system, a subsystem, a sub-subsystem or an assembly.
element.title: SNS code

element.name: snsDescr
element.definition: The element <snsDescr> provides a description of each specific SNS system.
element.title: SNS description

element.name: snsRules
element.definition: The element <snsRules> contains descriptions of one or several SNS systems that apply to the project concerned.
element.title: SNS rules

element.name: snsSubSubsystem
element.definition: The element <snsSubSubsystem> contains the specification of each sub-subsystem defined for the SNS subsystem concerned.
element.title: SNS sub-subsystem code

element.name: snsSubSystem
element.definition: The element <snsSubSystem> contains the specification of each subsystem defined for the SNS system.
element.title: SNS subsystem code

element.name: snsSystem
element.definition: The element <snsSystem>contains the specification of each system defined for the SNS.
element.title: SNS system code

element.name: snsTitle
element.definition: The element <snsTitle> contains a title corresponding to a code used to define a component in an SNS structure. The title is represented as text content. Depending on the context it can be either the title of a system, a subsystem, a sub-subsystem or an assembly.
element.title: SNS title

element.name: softwareClassification
element.definition: The element <softwareClassification> is used for software classification as defined by the regulation authorities.
element.title: Software classification

element.name: softwareCustomizationStatus
element.definition: The element <softwareCustomizationStatus> indicates if the software can be updated or customized by the operator.
element.title: Software customization status

element.name: solderSleeve
element.definition:
The element <solderSleeve> is contained in the list of solder sleeves element <solderSleeveGroup>. The list of solder sleeves is a child element of the list of the standard parts element <standardPartGroup>.
It contains information concerning the properties of solder sleeves used in the Product's wiring.
element.title: Description of element <solderSleeve> and child elements

element.name: solderSleeveGroup
element.definition: the list of solder sleeves
element.title: Electrical standard parts

element.name: source
element.definition:
A related resource from which the described resource is derived. Typically the element <dc:source> will contain the derivative source for the described resource. If the element <dc:source> is absent, no assumptions can be made about the status of derived content.
S1000D maps the data module or publication module derivative classification source to the Dublin core element, ie, the value of element <derivativeSource>.
element.title: Dublin core elements

element.name: sourceDmIdent
element.definition: The element <sourceDmIdent> contains the identification of the source data module on which the current data module instance is based.
element.title: Source data module identification

element.name: sourceDocRef
element.definition: The element <sourceDocRef> indicates where the information for the BRDP is defined. This reference can point to a place inside the same business rules data module, a data module with related business rule information or external documents in a non-S1000D or non-XML format.
element.title: Business rules document reference

element.name: sourceMaintRecoverability
element.definition: The element <sourceMaintRecoverability> identifies maintenance activities which are performed on an item.
element.title: Source maintenance and recoverability

element.name: sourcePmIdent
element.definition: The element <sourcePmIdent> contains the source publication module identification information such as publication module code, language and issue information.
element.title: Source publication module identification

element.name: sourceScormContentPackageIdent
element.definition: The element <sourceScormContentPackageIdent> contains the identification information of the source SCORM content package module on which the SCORM content package module is based.
element.title: Source SCORM content package module identification

element.name: sourceType
element.definition: The element <sourceType> contains information of the source of the requirement. This gives the ability to include requirements such as failure effect category, failure effect code, Enhanced Zonal Analysis Program (EZAP), Lightning or High Intensity Radiated Field (L/HIRF), CMR 1 star, CMR 2 star, and future regulatory sources.
element.title: Source type

element.name: sourcingType
element.definition: The element <sourcingType> indicates how the part can be procured. It can be used, for example, to indicate whether the part is a BFE part or a seller furnished equipment (SFE) part. It can also be used to indicate if the part sourcing is supervised by a specific contract. This value can be customer-dependent so the attribute applicRefId can be used to specify the customer(s) for which the information is valid.
element.title: Sourcing type

element.name: spanspec
element.definition: The element <spanspec> (span specification) identifies a horizontal span of columns and associated attributes that can subsequently be referenced by its attribute spanname for repeated use in a table’s <entry> elements. A <spanspec> uses column names from <colspec> elements defined in that context. It gets default values from the <colspec> that provides the namest. namest and nameend identify the first and last columns in increasing left-to-right order that identify the span. The reason colname is used rather than colnum in identifying <spanspec> is that the names are independent of revisions that can change the number of inserted/deleted columns, as long as namest remains to the left of (has a smaller column than) nameend. In a <thead> or <tfoot>, if any <colspec> are redefined, the <tgroup> <spanspec> elements are ignored in references to spanname from <entry> elements within this <thead> or <tfoot>. When such redefinition occurs, any spanning should be by the attributes namest and nameend.
element.title: Span specification

element.name: spareDescr
element.definition:
The element <spareDescr> contains the information on each item of spare or set of spares.
The attribute id in the element <spareDescr> can be applied so that the spares can be cross-referenced from the procedure.
element.title: Spare description

element.name: spareDescrGroup
element.definition: container element for spares
element.title: preliminary requirements

element.name: sparePartClass
element.definition: The element <sparePartClass> contains the spare parts classification (SPC).
element.title: Spare parts classification

element.name: specDocument
element.definition: The element <specDocument> contains identifications of the part specifications. This includes information such as drawing numbers for manufacturer parts and standard specification numbers for standard parts.
element.title: Specification

element.name: specGroup
element.definition: The element <specGroup> contains the specification that the supply meets.
element.title: Specification list

element.name: specialConnection
element.definition:
The element <specialConnection> contains a numerical value and must be used for all wires, that are connected to "special contacts" of an equipment such as stowed wires and wires connected to the shell of this equipment.
In the graphical presentation, these wires are always, depending on the given value, shown after the wires, which are connected to real contacts of the equipment.
Wires with the wire state value "logconn", which are connected to special contacts (eg, when a shield is electrically connected to the shell of a connector by the cable clamp that is represented by the logical connection wire), must not get allocated data in element <specialConnection>.
element.title: Special connections

element.name: specialStorage
element.definition: The element <specialStorage> indicates whether an item requires special storage. <specialStorage>0</specialStorage> indicates that the item does not require special storage. <specialStorage>1</specialStorage> indicates that the item has special storage requirements. If the content of <specialStorage> is set to "1" then the content of element <reasonForSelection> must not be "0".
element.title: Special storage

element.name: specialTerminal
element.definition: The element <specialTerminal> identifies special terminals. It is recommended to define all special terminals and to identify them by populating this element with the value "Y". If the contact is not a special terminal, this element can be populated with the value "N", or not used at all.
element.title: Special terminal information

element.name: sru
element.definition: The element <sru> contains a name, a shortname and an identification of the SRU.
element.title: SRU identification

element.name: sruItem
element.definition: The element <sruItem> contains the information needed to perform the test on the faulty SRU.
element.title: Fault isolation test - SRU

element.name: standardPartGroup
element.definition: The element <standardPartGroup> is used as a container for the different categories of electrical standard parts data.
element.title: Electrical standard parts

element.name: state
element.definition: The element <state> contains the state where the business is located.
element.title: State

element.name: street
element.definition: The element <street> contains the street address of the business.
element.title: Street

element.name: stringFunction
element.definition:
The element <stringFunction> is a string function that evaluates the element <expression> based on the attribute "stringAction" with the following definitions–
• "empty" – Returns "true" if the operand is empty otherwise returns "false".
• "sizeOf" – Returns an integer value that is the number of characters in the operand.
element.title: Information processing

element.name: stringOperator
element.definition:
The element <stringOperator> is an operator between two string expressions in the Process DM. The operator evaluates the two element <expression>s based on the attribute "stringOperation" with following definitions–
• "concatenate" – Returns a string data type that is the first string with the second string concatenated.
• "contains" – Returns a Boolean data type "true" if the first string occurs anywhere within the second string otherwise returns "false" otherwise.
• "equal" – Returns a Boolean data type "true" if the values are the identical otherwise returns "false".
• "greaterThan" – Returns a Boolean data type "true" if the first string is greater than the second string otherwise returns "false".
• "greaterThanOrEqual" – Returns a Boolean data type "true" if the first string is greater than or equal to the second string otherwise returns "false"
• "lessThan" – Returns a Boolean data type "true" if the first string is less than the second string otherwise returns "false"
• "lessThanOrEqual" – Returns a Boolean data type "true" if the first string is less than or equal to the second string otherwise returns "false"
• "notEqual" – Returns a Boolean data type "true" if the values are not identical otherwise returns "false".
element.title: Process data module - Logic engine

element.name: stringValue
element.definition: The element <stringValue> is an expression operand that is set to the data type string. A string is a sequence of characters.
element.title: values

element.name: structureObjectRule
element.definition: The element <structureObjectRule> contains rules that apply to an element or an attribute, which must be given when the intended use of that object does not fully coincide with the specification, for instance by being more restrictive.
element.title: Structure object rule

element.name: structureObjectRuleGroup
element.definition: The element <structureObjectRuleGroup> specifies all rules about the project-specific use of elements and attributes for all the various document types (Schema) used within the project.
element.title: Structure rules

element.name: subCrewDrill
element.definition: The element <subCrewDrill> contains a subdrill, which is part of a crew/operator drill.
element.title: Subdrill statement

element.name: subject
element.definition:
The topic of the content of the resource. Typically, a subject will be expressed as keywords, key phrases or classification codes that describe a topic of the resource. Recommended best practice is to select a value from a controlled vocabulary or formal classification scheme.
S1000D maps the data module title or the publication module title or the SCORM content package module title to this Dublin core element.
element.title: Dublin core elements

element.name: subjectIdent
element.definition: The element <subjectIdent> contains the subject's identification (SID) and provides identification of the item, or items, which are the subjects of the IPPN.
element.title: Subject identification

element.name: subjectVariantSegment
element.definition:
The element <subjectVariantSegment> identifies the S2000M subject variant segment (VAS). It is an extension of the IPPN to store information regarding the identification (part number, manufacturer (CAGE code) and NATO stock number) of the subject for which the IPP has been prepared. In cases where the IPP presents data against variants of the subject, the element <subjectVariantSegment> is repeated the appropriate number of times to hold all the variants' identifications.
If the element <subjectVariantSegment> is not used, then the corresponding information can be retrieved from the first data module (figure) of the IPP which means from all first CSN. In the formatted CSN, these are the digits 10 thru 13 for the six character SNS and 13 thru 16 for the nine character SNS. These four digits represent the item number (given by the attribute item) and the item number variant (given by the attribute itemVariant).
element.title: Subject variant

element.name: subScript
element.definition: The element <subScript> contains the text that is intended to be presented subscripted.
element.title: Subscript

element.name: subStringFunction
element.definition: The element <subStringFunction> is a string function that evaluates the element <expression> to return a substring based on the children elements <subStringPosition> and <subStringLength>.
element.title: Information processing

element.name: subStringLength
element.definition:
The element <subStringLength> is a parameter to the element <subStringFunction> that specifies the number characters. The integer value has the following definitions–
• A positive value moves the length towards the string end.
• A negative value moves the length towards the string start.
• A zero value moves the length to the string end.
• A positive value greater then remainder of the string moves the length to the string end.
• A negative value less then start of the string moves the length to the string start.
element.title: Information processing

element.name: subStringPosition
element.definition:
The element <subStringPosition> is a parameter to the element <subStringFunction> that specifies the position in the string. The integer value has the following definitions–
• A positive integer is positioned from the start of the string.
• A negative integer is positioned from the end of the string.
• A zero integer is positioned at the end of the string.
element.title: Information processing

element.name: superScript
element.definition:
The element <superScript> contains the text that is intended to be presented superscripted.
Note
Superscript is the default value for inline and table footnotes.
element.title: Superscript

element.name: supersedure
element.definition: The element <supersedure> contains a notice that the data module supersedes other data modules. For example, the requirements that must be applied to a data module with regard to supersedure notices.
element.title: Supersedure

element.name: supervisorLevel
element.definition: The element <supervisorLevel> contains the hierarchical level in the supervisory structure that explains if the supervisor must observe the task or inspect the results of the task.
element.title: Supervisor level

element.name: supplyClassification
element.definition: The element <supplyClassification> contains a classification related to the supply. For example, it can be used to provide a rating of the supply eco-efficiency.
element.title: Supply classification

element.name: supplyDescr
element.definition:
The element <supplyDescr> contains the information on each item of supply or set of supplies.
The attribute id in the element <supplyDescr> can be applied so that the supply can be cross-referenced from the procedure.
element.title: Supply description

element.name: supplyDescrGroup
element.definition: container element for supplies
element.title: preliminary requirements

element.name: supplyIdent
element.definition: The element <supplyIdent>, contrary to the attribute id, semantically identifies the supply as a result of its attributes.
element.title: Supply identifier

element.name: supplyRef
element.definition: The element <supplyRef> contains the references to a supply, for those projects that use a supplies common information repository. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.11.8.
element.title: Supply reference

element.name: supplyRepository
element.definition: The element <supplyRepository> provides a list of supplies and their associated properties.
element.title: Supplies repository

element.name: supplyRqmt
element.definition: The element <supplyRqmt> represents one alternative of the supply requirement. Each alternative lists one or several supplies that fulfill the supply requirement.
element.title: Alternative of the supply requirement

element.name: supplyRqmtAltIdent
element.definition: The element <supplyRqmtAltIdent>, contrary to the attribute id, semantically identifies the supply requirement as a result of its attributes.
element.title: Supply requirement alternative identifier

element.name: supplyRqmtAlts
element.definition: The element <supplyRqmtAlts> contains a group of supply requirement alternatives.
element.title: Supply requirement alternative group

element.name: supplyRqmtAltStatus
element.definition: The element <supplyRqmtAltStatus> tracks the supply requirement alternative history. For example, it indicates the supply requirement alternative authorization status and indicates if it has been superseded by another supply requirement or split into two other supply requirements.
element.title: Supply requirement alternative status

element.name: supplyRqmtIdent
element.definition: The element <supplyRqmtIdent>, contrary to the attribute id, semantically identifies the supply requirement as a result of its attributes.
element.title: Supply requirement identifier

element.name: supplyRqmtRef
element.definition: The element <supplyRqmtRef> contains the reference to a consumable, material and expendable for projects which manage a common information repository.
element.title: Supply requirement reference

element.name: supplyRqmtRepository
element.definition: The element <supplyRqmtRepository> contains a list of supply requirements and their associated properties.
element.title: Supply requirement repository

element.name: supplyRqmtSpec
element.definition: The element <supplyRqmtSpec> contains the associated properties of one supply requirement.
element.title: Supply requirement specification

element.name: supplyRqmtStatus
element.definition: The element <supplyRqmtStatus> contains the supply requirement history. For instance, it indicates the supply requirement authorization status, if it has been superseded by another one, or split into two other supply requirements.
element.title: Supply requirement status

element.name: supplySet
element.definition: The element <supplySet> identifies supplies that must be used together (eg, base and primer supply).
element.title: Supply set

element.name: supplySetGroup
element.definition: The element <supplySetGroup> is constituted of supplies and/or sets of supplies. Each supply or set of supplies can be used equally.
element.title: Supply set group

element.name: supplySpec
element.definition: The element <supplySpec> contains the associated properties for one supply.
element.title: Supply specification

element.name: supportEquipDescr
element.definition:
The element <supportEquipDescr> contains the information on each item of support equipment.
The attribute id in the element <supportEquipDescr> can be applied so that the support equipment can be cross-referenced from the procedure.
element.title: Support equipment description

element.name: supportEquipDescrGroup
element.definition: container element for support equipment
element.title: preliminary requirements

element.name: surfaceProtection
element.definition: The element <surfaceProtection> contains the protection information applied to the surface of a distribution part.
element.title: Protection

element.name: symbol
element.definition: The element <symbol> contains illustrations and graphics that are intended to be presented in line within the normal text. Symbols must be controlled using the ICN as described in Chap 4.4. However, the ICN must not be presented.
element.title: Symbol

element.name: systemBreakdownCode
element.definition: The element <systemBreakdownCode> contains a code that captures the physical breakdown for those projects that are producing data modules that are in line with some logistic databases. This element can be used to provide the system breakdown code of a complex equipment which is divided into subsystems.
element.title: System breakdown code

element.name: systemIdent
element.definition: the discriminating identification information to identify the faulty system (eg, "yellow" for a hydraulic flag system)
element.title: Detailed description of the isolated fault

element.name: systemLocation
element.definition: the location or panel where the malfunction is shown
element.title: Detailed description of the isolated fault

element.name: systemName
element.definition: the faulty system
element.title: Detailed description of the isolated fault

element.name: systemPosition
element.definition: the discriminating identification information, position-oriented (eg, "left")
element.title: Detailed description of the isolated fault

element.name: table
element.definition: The element <table> is used to contain information which is to be presented in a tabular form. The element is used for both formal and informal tables.
element.title: Table markup

element.name: targetDmIssueInfo
element.definition: The issue information of the target data module, which is populated in the same way as normal data modules.
element.title: Data update file status

element.name: targetDmStatus
element.definition: The status of the target data module to be retrieved from the data update file. This element contains the same elements and attributes as the status section of a normal data module.
element.title: Data update file status

element.name: task
element.definition: The element <task> contains general data for a task that belongs to an inspection.
element.title: Task

element.name: taskCategory
element.definition: The element <taskCategory> specifies the usage of the support equipment (eg, servicing, maintenance, overhaul, repair).
element.title: Task code

element.name: taskDefinition
element.definition: The element <taskDefinition> contains the necessary information to define a planned maintenance task.
element.title: Task definition

element.name: taskDefinitionAlts
element.definition: The element <taskDefinitionAlts> provides the capability to group several alternate solutions of the task information necessary to plan maintenance. Refer to Chap 4.13.3 for information related to the use of alternates group in data module content.
element.title: Task definition alternates group

element.name: taskDescr
element.definition: The element <taskDescr> contains the task description.
element.title: Task description

element.name: taskDuration
element.definition:
The element <taskDuration> contains the elapsed times for performing the actions specified in Preliminary requirements, the Main procedure and the Requirements after job completion which are necessary to achieve the complete maintenance action.
Note
All referenced actions must be included.
element.title: Maintenance task duration

element.name: taskGroup
element.definition: The element <taskGroup> contains a list of tasks that belong to the inspection. The element <taskGroup> facilitates production of a table of tasks in an inspection.
element.title: Task group

element.name: taskItem
element.definition: The element <taskItem> contains an individual task that belongs to an inspection.
element.title: Task item

element.name: taskMarker
element.definition: The element <taskMarker> contains the marker to separate an aggregation of text that came from regulatory sources into discretely classified textual items.
element.title: Task marker

element.name: taskTitle
element.definition: the task title.
element.title: task

element.name: tbody
element.definition: The element <tbody> (table body) contains the rows of the table that provide the primary content and is mandatory for all tables. The table body must contain at least one table row.
element.title: Table body

element.name: techData
element.definition: The element <techData> contains the technical properties related to the part.
element.title: Technical data

element.name: techName
element.definition:
The element <techName> content must reflect the name of the hardware or function. That is, it must reflect the system, subsystem or sub-subsystem concerned based on the Product breakdown.
Examples:
element.title: Technical name

element.name: techPubBase
element.definition: The element <techPubBase> contains the technical publication baseline to which the data module is written. The publication baseline number represents the technical build standard of the Product, which defines the technical standard by reference to a certain point in time. This is achieved by declaring a "Cut-off date" or milestone and listing the drawing set standard, relevant modification documents, etc, issued prior to the Cut-off or milestone date and used as source material for the production of data modules. The technical standard so defined, ensures that all data modules are to the same standard. Projects can also decide to use this element to record the delivery number of the Logistics Support Analysis Record (LSAR) to which the data module is written.
element.title: Publication baseline number

element.name: techStandard
element.definition: The element <techStandard> contains the authorized technical standard and associated data to be entered, describing the information basis used to write the data module. The information basis can be described by giving, for example, the modification or drawing standard including amendments, alterations, etc, or a publication baseline. Technical standard, in this context, is defined as the technical design and development level of the technical information of the Product, on which the data module has been based. This element contains authority block or technical publications baseline number or both.
element.title: Technical standard

element.name: temperature
element.definition: The element <temperature> describes the minimum and maximum temperature rating for which the harness, the distribution part, the shrink sleeve, the conduit or the wire material is approved.
element.title: Harness temperature rating

element.name: terminationModuleGroupingValue
element.definition:
The element <terminationModuleGroupingValue> is only used for equipment with internal logic. It provides grouping information for contacts that belong to the same module of a termination module assembly.
It must contain an identical alphanumerical value for all these contacts.
element.title: Termination module grouping information

element.name: termTitle
element.definition: The element <termTitle> contains the column title for the terms.
element.title: Term title

element.name: testDescr
element.definition: The element <taskDescr> contains the task description.
element.title: Task description

element.name: testName
element.definition: Fault isolation test performance
element.title: the name of the test

element.name: testParameters
element.definition: The element <testParameters> contains a set of attributes to capture parameters for the test.
element.title: Test parameters

element.name: testProcedure
element.definition: Fault isolation test performance
element.title: the reference to test procedures or any relevant information

element.name: tfoot
element.definition: The element <tfoot> (table footer) contains the rows and entries that represent the table footer. The table footer is to appear at the end of the table (ie, after the last table body row). For page-oriented publications, the table footer is to appear after the last row of each page on which the table appears.
element.title: Table footer

element.name: tgroup
element.definition: A table can contain one or more table group <tgroup> elements. Normally, there will be one table group. Multiple table groups are used to define portions of the table that have different presentation requirements (eg, column and row specifications).
element.title: Table group

element.name: thead
element.definition: The element <thead> (table header) contains the rows of the table that provide the column headings and is mandatory for S1000D formal tables. The table header is displayed in bold type face and, for page-oriented output, will be displayed at the top of each page on which the table appears. The table header must contain at least one table row. There is no need to use emphasis to make a table header bold.
element.title: Table header

element.name: thermoCoupleMinus
element.definition: The element <thermoCoupleMinus> identifies the negative contact of thermocouples. It is recommended to identify thermocouple contacts by populating this element with the value "Y". If the contact is not a thermocouple minus, this element can be populated with the value "N", or not used at all.
element.title: Thermocouple minus

element.name: thermoCouplePlus
element.definition: The element <thermoCouplePlus> identifies the positive contact of thermocouples. It is recommended to identify thermocouple contacts by populating this element with the value "Y". If the contact is not a thermocouple plus, this element can be populated with the value "N", or not used at all.
element.title: Thermocouple plus

element.name: threshold
element.definition: The element <threshold> contains the limit information in terms of a value before which the inspection must be performed. The value can be given in calendar time or in other terms such as the number of hours a Product has been operating.
element.title: Threshold information

element.name: thresholdInterval
element.definition:
The element <thresholdInterval> contains any (one or more) threshold interval information that is relevant to the task. It is populated in the format of threshold or threshold interval value. For example 25 followed by the value of the attribute thresholdUnitOfMeasure (eg, FH).
Recommendation: The use of the element <thresholdInterval> can conflict with the same data given in the maintenance planning information data modules. A close coordination must be taken if the same data is given in both the procedure and the maintenance planning information. It is recommended to use the maintenance planning information in preference of the element <thresholdInterval> in each procedural data module.
element.title: Threshold interval

element.name: thresholdValue
element.title: the value before which the inspection must be performed. The element <thresholdValue> has only textual content.

element.name: thumbTabText
element.definition: The element <thumbTabText> contains thumb tab text for the drill or subdrill.
element.title: Tab text

element.name: timeLimit
element.definition: The element <timeLimit> contains a time limit.
element.title: Time limit

element.name: timeLimitCategory
element.definition: The element <timeLimitCategory> is used to classify the time limit.
element.title: Time limit category

element.name: timeLimitInfo
element.definition: The element <timeLimitInfo> contains time limits, periodicities and life details information relating to equipment or systems.
element.title: Time limit information

element.name: title
element.definition:
The element <title> contains the title for a paragraph, step, table or figure, etc. Titles can also be used in the preparation of Table of Contents (TOC). Doing this enables the level of indenture in the TOC to be controlled.
In descriptive data modules, titles can be included for the element <levelledPara> from sublevel one thru five, but must be included for element <figure> and element <table> for formal tables. Refer to Chap 6.2.2.
In procedural, process, crew and fault data modules, titles must be included for <figure> and <table> for formal tables. Refer to default BREX rule BREX-S1-00068. Titles can also be included for the element <proceduralStep> from sublevel one thru five and for the element <dmNode>. Refer to Chap 6.2.2.
All titles must be presented in sentence case. Refer to Chap 3.9.1.
element.title: Titles

element.name: tocEntry
element.definition:
The element <tocEntry> contains the document title, document code, issue information, number of pages and applicability for each entry in the Table of contents. It is also used to build the title hierarchy by only using the elements <title> and <tocEntry>.
The content is authored or derived from the source publication module.
Note
The titles can be derived from the element <pmEntryTitle> in the source publication module and replicate the hierarchy therein.
element.title: Table of contents entry

element.name: tocList
element.definition:
The element <tocList> contains the list of all documents (data modules, publication modules and non-S1000D publications) in the publication.
The list entries must be given in the order of appearance in the publication.
element.title: Table of contents list

element.name: toEquip
element.definition: The content model electrical equipment defines the child elements of the elements <fromEquip> and <toEquip>, which contain the wire connection information for the two wire ends.
element.title: Content model electrical equipment

element.name: tolerance
element.definition: The element <tolerance> contains the threshold minimum and maximum values between which the inspection must be performed.
element.title: Tolerance information

element.name: tool
element.definition: The element <tool> contains information that describes the support equipment and provides further information related to the support equipment based on the Product configuration.
element.title: Alternate support equipment

element.name: toolAlts
element.definition: The element <toolAlts> is a group of support equipment alternates.
element.title: Support equipment alternate group

element.name: toolIdent
element.definition: The element <toolIdent>, contrary to the attribute id, semantically identifies the support equipment as a result of its attributes.
element.title: Support equipment identifier

element.name: toolRef
element.definition: The element <toolRef> contains the reference to support equipment (standard tool, special tool or test equipment), for those projects that use a tool common information repository.
element.title: Tool reference

element.name: toolRefGroup
element.definition: The element <toolRefGroup> provides relationships and the types of relationships with other support equipment.
element.title: References to other support equipment

element.name: toolRepository
element.definition: The element <toolRepository> contains a list of support equipment and their associated properties.
element.title: Support equipment repository

element.name: toolsList
element.definition: The element <toolsList> contains a list of support equipment (special tools, standard tools and test equipment) required to maintain the equipment as indicated in the maintenance allocation. When support equipment is indicated in the maintenance allocation, the tools list must be included.
element.title: Tools list

element.name: toolsListCode
element.definition: Tools list
element.title: the identification for the purposes of cross-referencing. The element <toolsListCode> also allows textual data in the event that a particular code such as "A", "B", or "C" is used to identify the support equipment in the maintenance allocation data.

element.name: toolsListGroup
element.definition: The element <toolsListGroup> contains the data about the support equipment. It includes the maintenance level, name of the support equipment, NATO stock number, and support equipment number.
element.title: Tools list group

element.name: toolSpec
element.definition:
The element <toolSpec> contains the associated properties for one piece of support equipment.
Some properties of the support equipment can vary from one product instance to another. Such properties are managed under the support equipment alternates (element <toolAlts>), which can contain a reference to an applicability annotation. Refer to Chap 4.13.3 for details on the alternates concept.
For some properties appearing at both levels (<toolSpec> and <tool>), the following rule must be applied: if the property is fulfilled in the alternate element, it supersedes the generic value stored in the parent element.
element.title: Support equipment specification

element.name: toolsRefs
element.definition: The element <toolsRefs> contains references to required support equipment (standard tools, special tools and test equipment) that is contained in the tools list.
element.title: Tools references

element.name: totalQuantity
element.definition: The element <totalQuantity> contains the total number of units of the subject part used in the complete assembly of the Product. This information can be used to build the alphanumerical index.
element.title: Total quantity

element.name: trade
element.definition: The element <trade> contains the typed in required trade code of the person. Care must be taken when using this element when the product is, for example, multi-national or multi-customer. The trade or trade code or both is typed in.
element.title: Trade/trade code

element.name: trainingStep
element.definition: The element <trainingStep> is used for the arrangement of SCO content data module content into sections of related training content to be presented as a unit.
element.title: Training step

element.name: transport
element.definition: The element <transport> contains information about the transport of the supply.
element.title: Transport

element.name: transverseLink
element.definition: The element <transverseLink> provides information about internally or externally realized electrical connections of different contacts/bolts of equipment (eg, terminal strips).
element.title: Transverse link

element.name: triaxialCableFlag
element.definition: The element <triaxialCableFlag> identifies tri-axial cables or contacts. In many cases, a tri-axial cable is indicated by the letter "Y" in the element content
element.title: Tri-axial information

element.name: trigger
element.definition: The element <trigger> contains the means to establish that the inspection must be performed after a task(s) or event(s).
element.title: Trigger

element.name: trueValue
element.definition: Process data module - Logic engine
element.title: The element <trueValue> is an expression operand that is set to "true" with the data type Boolean.

element.name: twist
element.definition:
The element <twist> and ist attribute twistingType contain information about the twisting of a wire or about the twisting of an extremity of the wire.
The attribute twistingType specifies the type of twisting in coded form.
Twist information can be different on both sides of a wire. The element <twist> must contain the twist information on this side of the wire.
element.title: Twist information

element.name: twistGroup
element.definition: The element <twistGroup> is used as a container for the twist information.
element.title: Twisting of twisted wires

element.name: type
element.definition:
The nature or genre of the content of the resource. Element <dc:type> includes terms describing general categories, functions, genres, or aggregation levels for content. Recommended best practice is to select a value from a controlled vocabulary (eg, the recommended list of Dublin core types). To describe the physical or digital manifestation of the resource, use the element <dc:format>.
S1000D assigns the fixed value "text" to this Dublin core element.
element.title: Dublin core elements

element.name: typeDesignation
element.definition: components
element.title: the textual information for additional information about the item.

element.name: unitOfIssue
element.definition: The element <unitOfIssue> contains the unit of issue (UOI) information. The unit of issue indicates the physical measurement, count or container in which the item is issued.
element.title: Unit of issue

element.name: unitOfIssueQualificationSegment
element.definition: The element <unitOfIssueQualificationSegment> identifies an S2000M unit of issue qualification segment (PCS).
element.title: PCS data

element.name: unverified
element.definition: The element <unverified> identifies that the data module has not yet been first verified.
element.title: Unverified

element.name: update
element.definition: The element <update> contains the data module fragments that have been added, deleted or modified since a previous issue of the target data module. A fragment can be updated only once in a data update file.
element.title: Updates

element.name: updateAddress
element.definition: The element <updateAddress> contains the identification and issue date of the data update file.
element.title: Data update file address

element.name: updateCode
element.definition: The element <updateCode> contains the code that identifies the data update file. The data update file code is based on the data module code of the target data module. For example, the data update file address of the part CIR data module must be based on the part CIR data module code (see markup example).
element.title: Data update file code

element.name: updateIdent
element.definition: The element <updateIdent> contains the unique identification of the data update file.
element.title: Data update file identification

element.name: updateIdentAndStatusSection
element.definition: The element <updateIdentAndStatusSection> is used to provide the data update file identification (data update file address), the data update file status and the status of the data module to be updated.
element.title: Data update file identification and status section

element.name: updateStatus
element.definition: The element <updateStatus> contains the status of the data update file. Among other information, the data update file status indicates from which data module the update was calculated.
element.title: Data update file status

element.name: usableOnCodeAssy
element.definition: The element <usableOnCodeAssy> contains the assembly variants and configuration variants to which the item is applied. The element <usableOnCodeAssy> is used for chapterized IPD.
element.title: Usable on code assembly

element.name: usableOnCodeEquip
element.definition: The element <usableOnCodeEquip> contains the equipment variants and configurations to which the item is applied. The element <usableOnCodeEquip> is used for non-chapterized IPD.
element.title: Usable on code equipment

element.name: usage
element.definition: The element <usage> contains the (alternative) textual description of the supply requirement usage.
element.title: Usage

element.name: userEntry
element.definition: A user entry dialog receives data input by the user.
element.title: User entry dialog

element.name: validate
element.definition:
The element <validate> is used for validating the user entry thru an expression evaluation of the input information. The validation authenticates both numerical and non-numerical input.
− Validating numerical user entry inputs
The numerical validation expression determines if the input value is in a specified number range. The expression uses standard numerical comparison operators (ie, equal, greaterThan, lessThanOrEqual) and connects the numerical comparisons with a Boolean connector (ie, or, and, exclusiveOr).
An example is to validate that a pressure is entered in the range from 0 to 90 p.s.i. A state variable Pressure is used and the validation expression is given as [[Pressure >= 0] and [Pressure <= 90]]. A user entry that is < 0 or > 90 indicates an invalid value.
− Validating non-numerical (character string) user entry inputs
The non-numerical validation expression can use various expression operators to determine the user entry length, included or excluded within a "set-string", or correct alpha or numerical text is entered in the correct sequence.
An example is to validate that five characters are entered for a CAGE code. A state variable CAGEC is used and the validation expression is given as [sizeOf (CAGEC) = 5]. A user entry that is less or more than five characters indicates an invalid value.
element.title: Validate user entry

element.name: valueAfterAction
element.definition: The element <valueAfterAction> contains a secondary classification action to occur on secondary date.
element.title: Value after action

element.name: variable
element.definition:
Variables define pieces of information required by the logic engine to context filter, branch, etc, within a process data module. Variables can be global or local in scope. Local variables are defined in the element <variable> described below. Local variables are only available to the process data module in which they are declared.
If a variable must be available to several process data modules, it must be declared in the ACT or CCT as a product attribute or condition. It will then be recognized by the logic engine as a global property and made available to all process data modules. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.3.1 and Chap 3.9.5.3.2 for descriptions of the element <productAttribute> and the element <condition>. More information is provided in Chap 4.14.1 and Chap 4.14.2 on these concepts.
In the process data module, the element <productAttribute> and the element <condition> can be used in the applicability structure as well as in expressions, assertions, etc. These elements are initialized in the PCT. They will not have a dialog as part of their content compared to local variables. However, they have a prompt available in their definitions which can be used to query the user for the value of the product attribute or condition as well as enumeration label attributes which can be used as menu choices in an IETP software constructed dialog. Dialogs, such as are defined for local variables, can also be authored for global properties on the element <dmNode> or the element <proceduralStep>.
element.title: Variables (local)

element.name: variableDeclarations
element.definition: Variable declarations define and can initialize one to many local variables that are used within the process data module.
element.title: Variable declarations (local)

element.name: variablePostSet
element.definition: The element <variablePostSet> mirrors the element <variablePreSet>. It contains an assertion and assigns a value to a variable after the element <dmNode> or the element <process> has been traversed. For example, a variable postSet could be used on a step to apply power to assert to the state table that power has been applied for future reference by the logic engine. Likewise, a variable postSet could be used on a process to record in the state table the fact that some component has been replaced which can be used downstream in the maintenance flow.
element.title: Variable postset

element.name: variablePreSet
element.definition: A child of both the element <process> and the element <dmNode>, the element <variablePreSet> assigns a value to a local variable or global property before its parent element is traversed. The element <variablePreSet> contains the element <assertion>. An assertion contains the element <variableRef> or <globalPropertyRef> and the element <expression>. The element <variableRef> and the element <globalPropertyRef> have attributes which uniquely define a local variable or global property. The element <variableRef> has the attribute variableName. The element <globalPropertyRef> has attributes applicPropertyIdent and applicPropertyType. When an element <variablePreSet> is encountered in the data, the logic engine evaluates the expression and assigns the resulting value to the local variable or global property identified by the element <variableRef> or the element <globalPropertyRef>. One use case is that the element <variablePreSet> is used on an element <dmNode> with an external application call in order to assign values to one or more of the parameter variables to be passed to the application. On the element <process>, it can be necessary to preset to "noValue" the value of a global property representing wind speed prior to beginning a procedure because the wind speed can have changed since the value of that property was last accessed.
element.title: Variable preset

element.name: variableRef
element.definition: The element <variableRef> points to a local variable.
element.title: Variable reference

element.name: verbatimText
element.definition:
The element <verbatimText> contains text that is supposed to be presented as is, without distorting the content. Typically, using the element <verbatimText>, the included text represents information such as computer program text or non-proportional display text.
When the element contains markup, the information must be entered in such a way that the marked up information does not interfere with the data module markup itself. One way to achieve this is to use a CDATA marked section for that information. This will also retain line feeds, etc.
element.title: Verbatim text

element.name: viewLocation
element.definition: Detailed description of the isolated fault
element.title: the location where the description of the fault is displayed

element.name: voltage
element.definition: The element <voltage> contains the voltage information of wire material.
element.title: Voltage

element.name: wallThickness
element.definition: The element <wallThickness> contains the information of the wall thickness of a conduit.
element.title: Wall thickness

element.name: warning
element.definition:
The element <warning> is used to contain hazard information that can cause personal injury or death.
Warnings in data modules must always precede the text where the hazard arises (for details see below) and must always precede cautions and notes.
Warnings of a general nature which are applicable throughout a procedure can precede the instruction to save repeating the warning before each of the individual steps of the maintenance task where the hazard arises. Refer to Para .
The default heading WARNING must begin the warning followed by symbols, if any.
Note
The default heading WARNING is not entered in the data modules when data modules are written in an XML editor or a What You See Is What You Get (WYSIWYG)-editor with an automatic function for presentation of legends. If not, the author must make sure that the default heading is included.
Warnings must not be numbered. Warnings must be the first item within that step/para.
Note
There are two methods to present warnings.
Note
Warnings must not be used for purely descriptive information (eg, data modules with information code 040). This does not prohibit the use of warnings in a descriptive data module for the production of content such as a summary of warnings.
Note
Warnings in business rules document data modules are strongly discouraged. The attribute brSeverityLevel must be used instead.
The following rule must be followed:
- Warnings that pertain to parent elements are also applicable to all of their child elements.
element.title: Warnings

element.name: warningAndCautionPara
element.definition: The element <warningAndCautionPara> contains the warning or caution text.
element.title: Warning and caution para

element.name: warningIdent
element.definition: The element <warningIdent> uniquely identifies the warning thru its attribute warningIdentNumber. This identifier must be used to refer from the data modules where the warning is externalized (element <warningRef>) to the externalized warning in the warnings CIR data module.
element.title: Warning identifier

element.name: warningMalfunction
element.definition: The basic fault (warning or malfunction) which is part of the current correlated fault.
element.title: Warning or malfunction

element.name: warningMalfunctionAlts
element.definition: The element <warningMalfunctionAlts> is an alternates group that provides the capability to group several alternate solutions of a basic fault (element <warningMalfunction>). Refer to Chap 4.13.3 for information related to the use of alternates group in data module content.
element.title: Warning or malfunction alternates group

element.name: warningRef
element.definition: The element <warningRef> contains the reference to a warning in a warnings CIR data module. It is used for externalizing warnings. Refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.11.13.
element.title: References to externalized warnings

element.name: warningRepository
element.definition: The element <warningRepository> contains a collection of warnings used in the data modules.
element.title: Warnings repository

element.name: warningsAndCautions
element.definition:
The element <warningsAndCautions> contains the collection of warnings and cautions used within a data module. Each warning or caution is authored separately within the element <warningsAndCautions>. Warnings and cautions can thus be authored once in a data module and then referenced as needed. The collection (element <warningsAndCautions>) must not include warnings or cautions that are not at all occur within the data module
. Note
The collection can be used in combination with warnings and cautions that are authored individually at the element level of a data module. The collection is useful to store common (or multi-use) alerts while local (or single-use) alerts can be authored as needed.
element.title: Internal collection

element.name: warningsAndCautionsRef
element.definition: The element <warningsAndCautionsRef> contains the references to the warnings or cautions, which are valid for this data module and stored externally into a warning and/or caution common information repository.
element.title: External collection

element.name: warningSpec
element.definition: The element <warningSpec> represents one warning.
element.title: Warning specification

element.name: wire
element.definition: The element <wire>, within the list of wires element <wireGroup>, contains wire information about the Product's wiring. The wires are the electrical connections between two equipment, different contacts of an equipment, etc. Electrical wires of a system are often bundled into harnesses.
element.title: Element <wire> and child elements

element.name: wireAlts
element.definition: The element <wireAlts>, within the element <wireGroup>, provides the capability to group several alternate solutions of wire data for different applicability annotations. Refer to Chap 4.13.3 for information related to the use of the alternates group mechanism in data module content.
element.title: Element <wireAlts>

element.name: wireCode
element.definition: The element <wireCode> gives information on the type of a wire and its gauge.
element.title: Wire code

element.name: wireColor
element.definition:
The element <wireColor> contains the color information of wire material, a distribution part, a shrink sleeve or a conduit.
Due to the fact that the <wireColor>, when containing the color information of wire material, is only for single use, the color of the cores of multiple core cables must be given in a single element <wireColor>.
When the <wireColor> contains the color information of a distribution part, it can be used to indicate the color codes (color bands) of contacts or splices.
element.title: Color information

element.name: wireConnection
element.definition: The element <wireConnection> contains connection information for the two ends of a wire. In special cases (eg, wires with wireState = "pigtail" or "notactiv") only the connection of one wire end is used.
element.title: Wire connection

element.name: wireConnectionCode
element.definition: The element <wireConnectionCode> is used for generic identification of the electrical potential to which a wire end is connected. This can be the basis to arrange the ends of the wires in a specific order for graphical display purposes and to generate a connection analysis.
element.title: Wire connection code

element.name: wireGauge
element.definition: The element <wireGauge> contains the gauge of a wire. The measure type is given in the attribute wireGaugeType.
element.title: Wire gauge

element.name: wireGroup
element.definition: wiring Data
element.title: the list of wires

element.name: wireIdent
element.definition: The element <wireIdent> is the unique identifier of a wire in the system.
element.title: Wire identification

element.name: wireInfo
element.definition: The element <wireInfo> contains child elements, which describe properties of the wire and other information related to the complete wire.
element.title: Wire information

element.name: wireInstallationCode
element.definition: The element <wireInstallationCode> contains the information for preparation and finishing of the wire for connection purposes.
element.title: Wire installation code

element.name: wireMaterial
element.definition:
The element <wireMaterial> is contained in the list of wire material element <wireMaterialGroup>. The list of wire material is a child element of the list of standard parts element <standardPartGroup>.
It contains information concerning the properties of wire material used in the Product's wiring.
element.title: Element <wireMaterial> and child elements

element.name: wireMaterialGroup
element.definition: Electrical standard parts
element.title: the list of wire materials

element.name: wireNumber
element.definition:
All wires must have a wire identification number. If there is no further breakdown, by the use of the circuit code element <circuitIdent> and the wire section identification element <sectionIdent>, then the element <wireNumber> must contain the complete wire identification.
If a conductor runs thru non-functional breaks (eg, a terminal junction module or a splice), the wire identification number must be the same for the two wires, but the section identifications are different.
element.title: Wire identification number

element.name: wireRoute
element.definition: The element <wireRoute> contains lane codes for wires, which belong to a primary system, and must be given a routing apart from other wires of the system.
element.title: Wire route lane code

element.name: wireSeqNumber
element.definition:
The element <wireSeqNumber> contains the sequential number of a wire, which can be part of the entire wire identification.
The wire sequential number must be used if the entire wire identification including its sequential number is not given in the element <wireNumber>.
For projects or organizations that hold the sequential wire number in the element <wireNumber> of the wire identification, the population of the element <wireSeqNumber> is not necessary.
element.title: Wire sequential number

element.name: wireType
element.definition: The element <wireType> gives information on the type of a wire. Normally, the wire type is defined in coded form.
element.title: Wire type

element.name: wiringData
element.definition: The element <wiringData> is used as a container for the lists of wires, harnesses, electrical equipment and electrical standard parts.
element.title: Element <wiringData>

element.name: wiringFields
element.definition: The wiring data description Schema defines the major element <wiringFields>, which contains the child elements for wire data/fields description, harness data/fields description, equipment data/fields description and standard parts data/fields description.
element.title: Element <wiringFields>

element.name: wiringPossibleCause
element.definition: The element <wiringPossibleCause> describes a wiring path that is a possible cause of a fault. It can also be used to identify a single electrical connection point. The element <wiringPossibleCause> contains the two ends of the wiring path.
element.title: Wiring path possible cause

element.name: wiringPossibleCauseFrom
element.definition: The wiring path extremity elements <wiringPossibleCauseFrom> and <wiringPossibleCauseTo> contain the wiring path connection information.
element.title: Wiring path extremities (from/to)

element.name: wiringPossibleCauseTo
element.definition: The wiring path extremity elements <wiringPossibleCauseFrom> and <wiringPossibleCauseTo> contain the wiring path connection information.
element.title: Wiring path extremities (from/to)

element.name: workArea
element.definition: The element <workArea> contains a descriptive identifier of the area where work is accomplished that cannot be deduced from the names of the collection of zones.
element.title: Work area

element.name: workAreaLocationGroup
element.definition: The element <workAreaLocationGroup> contains the area where work must be done. This area is sorted by zone, access point and work location.
element.title: Work area location group

element.name: workLocation
element.definition: The element <workLocation> contains the precise location where work must be done.
element.title: Work location

element.name: yesAnswer
element.definition: The element <yesAnswer> provides the internal link to the next action related to a positive answer.
element.title: Yes answer

element.name: yesNoAnswer
element.definition: The element <yesNoAnswer> contains the selection of the two answers Yes (element <yesAnswer>) or No (element <noAnswer>).
element.title: Answers - Yes or No

element.name: zone
element.definition: The element <zone> contains information that describes the zone and provides further information related to the zone based on the Product configuration.
element.title: Alternate zone

element.name: zoneAlts
element.definition: The element <zoneAlts> is a group of zone alternates.
element.title: Zone alternate group

element.name: zoneGroup
element.definition: The element <zoneGroup> contains the zoning information of a figure, in order to know where components, described in a figure, are installed on the Product. For instance, in case of damage, this enables that all figures impacted by the damage can easily be identified.
element.title: Zones

element.name: zoneIdent
element.definition: The element <zoneIdent>, contrary to the attribute id, semantically identifies the zone as a result of its attributes.
element.title: Zone identifier

element.name: zoneRef
element.definition:
The element <zoneRef> contains the zoning information (one or more), as described in Chap 3.4. For the use of the element <zoneRef> in connection with common information repositories, refer to Chap 4.13.1.
This element is available in the product management data within the element <productionMaintData> of preliminary requirements (refer to Chap 3.9.5.2.1.9) and within the element <para> of descriptive data modules.
element.title: Zone

element.name: zoneRefGroup
element.definition:
The element <zoneRefGroup> provides relationships and the types of relationships with other zones (eg, subzones). A proper use of this element enables the retrieval the zone breakdown (major zones that contain subzones that contain specific zones).
The types of relationships depend on the configuration of the Product. As a result, this element can be found at both the container and the alternate levels.
element.title: References to other zones

element.name: zoneRepository
element.definition: The element <zoneRepository> provides a list of zones and their associated properties.
element.title: Zone repository

element.name: zoneSide
element.definition: The element <zoneSide> indicates on which side of the Product the zone is located.
element.title: Zone side

element.name: zoneSpec
element.definition:
The element <zoneSpec> contains the associated properties of one zone.
Some properties of the zone can vary from one product instance to another. Such properties are managed under the zone alternates (element <zoneAlts>), which can contain a reference to an applicability annotation. Refer to Chap 4.13.3 for details on the alternates concept.
For some properties appearing at both levels, the following rule must be applied: if the property is fulfilled in the alternate element, it supersedes the generic value stored in the parent element.
element.title: Zone specification
